267	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	 * listpackEx functions
269	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
270	/*
271	 * If any of hash field expiration command is called on a listpack hash object
272	 * for the first time, we convert it to OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX encoding.
273	 * We allocate "struct listpackEx" which holds listpack pointer and expiry
274	 * metadata. In the listpack string, we append another TTL entry for each field
275	 * value pair. From now on, listpack will have triplets in it: field-value-ttl.
276	 * If TTL is not set for a field, we store 'zero' as the TTL value. 'zero' is
277	 * encoded as two bytes in the listpack. Memory overhead of a non-existing TTL
278	 * will be two bytes per field.
279	 *
280	 * Fields in the listpack will be ordered by TTL. Field with the smallest expiry
281	 * time will be the first item. Fields without TTL will be at the end of the
282	 * listpack. This way, it is easier/faster to find expired items.
283	 */
284	
285	#define HASH_LP_NO_TTL 0
286	
287	struct listpackEx *listpackExCreate(void) {
288	    listpackEx *lpt = zcalloc(sizeof(*lpt));
289	    lpt->meta.trash = 1;
290	    lpt->lp = NULL;
291	    return lpt;
292	}
293	
294	static void listpackExFree(listpackEx *lpt) {
295	    lpFree(lpt->lp);
296	    zfree(lpt);
297	}
298	
299	struct lpFingArgs {
300	    uint64_t max_to_search; /* [in] Max number of tuples to search */
301	    uint64_t expire_time;   /* [in] Find the tuple that has a TTL larger than expire_time */
302	    unsigned char *p;       /* [out] First item of the tuple that has a TTL larger than expire_time */
303	    int expired;            /* [out] Number of tuples that have TTLs less than expire_time */
304	    int index;              /* Internally used */
305	    unsigned char *fptr;    /* Internally used, temp ptr */
306	};
307	
308	/* Callback for lpFindCb(). Used to find number of expired fields as part of
309	 * active expiry or when trying to find the position for the new field according
310	 * to its expiry time.*/
311	static int cbFindInListpack(const unsigned char *lp, unsigned char *p,
312	                            void *user, unsigned char *s, long long slen)
313	{
314	    (void) lp;
315	    struct lpFingArgs *r = user;
316	
317	    r->index++;
318	
319	    if (r->max_to_search == 0)
320	        return 0; /* Break the loop and return */
321	
322	    if (r->index % 3 == 1) {
323	        r->fptr = p;  /* First item of the tuple. */
324	    } else if (r->index % 3 == 0) {
325	        serverAssert(!s);
326	
327	        /* Third item of a tuple is expiry time */
328	        if (slen == HASH_LP_NO_TTL || (uint64_t) slen >= r->expire_time) {
329	            r->p = r->fptr;
330	            return 0; /* Break the loop and return */
331	        }
332	        r->expired++;
333	        r->max_to_search--;
334	    }
335	
336	    return 1;
337	}
338	
339	/* Returns number of expired fields. */
340	static uint64_t listpackExExpireDryRun(const robj *o) {
341	    serverAssert(o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX);
342	
343	    listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
344	
345	    struct lpFingArgs r = {
346	        .max_to_search = UINT64_MAX,
347	        .expire_time = commandTimeSnapshot(),
348	    };
349	
350	    lpFindCb(lpt->lp, NULL, &r, cbFindInListpack, 0);
351	    return r.expired;
352	}
353	
354	/* Returns the expiration time of the item with the nearest expiration. */
355	static uint64_t listpackExGetMinExpire(robj *o) {
356	    serverAssert(o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX);
357	
358	    long long expireAt;
359	    unsigned char *fptr;
360	    listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
361	
362	    /* As fields are ordered by expire time, first field will have the smallest
363	     * expiry time. Third element is the expiry time of the first field */
364	    fptr = lpSeek(lpt->lp, 2);
365	    if (fptr != NULL) {
366	        serverAssert(lpGetIntegerValue(fptr, &expireAt));
367	
368	        /* Check if this is a non-volatile field. */
369	        if (expireAt != HASH_LP_NO_TTL)
370	            return expireAt;
371	    }
372	
373	    return EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
374	}
375	
376	/* Walk over fields and delete the expired ones. */
377	void listpackExExpire(redisDb *db, kvobj *kv, ExpireInfo *info) {
378	    OnFieldExpireCtx *ctx = info->ctx;
379	    serverAssert(kv->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX);
380	    uint64_t expired = 0, min = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
381	    unsigned char *ptr;
382	    listpackEx *lpt = kv->ptr;
383	
384	    ptr = lpFirst(lpt->lp);
385	
386	    sds key = kvobjGetKey(kv);
387	
388	    while (ptr != NULL && (info->itemsExpired < info->maxToExpire)) {
389	        long long val;
390	        int64_t flen;
391	        unsigned char intbuf[LP_INTBUF_SIZE], *fref;
392	
393	        fref = lpGet(ptr, &flen, intbuf);
394	
395	        ptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, ptr);
396	        serverAssert(ptr);
397	        ptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, ptr);
398	        serverAssert(ptr && lpGetIntegerValue(ptr, &val));
399	
400	        /* Fields are ordered by expiry time. If we reached to a non-expired
401	         * or a non-volatile field, we know rest is not yet expired. */
402	        if (val == HASH_LP_NO_TTL || (uint64_t) val > info->now)
403	            break;
404	
405	        /* Collect expired field for subkey notification. */
406	        if (ctx->vexpired) {
407	            char *fstr = (char *)(fref ? fref : intbuf);
408	            vecPush(ctx->vexpired, createStringObject(fstr, flen));
409	        }
410	
411	        propagateHashFieldDeletion(db, key, (char *)((fref) ? fref : intbuf), flen);
412	        server.stat_expired_subkeys++;
413	        if (ctx->activeEx) server.stat_expired_subkeys_active++;
414	
415	        ptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, ptr);
416	
417	        info->itemsExpired++;
418	        expired++;
419	    }
420	
421	    if (expired) {
422	        size_t oldsize = 0;
423	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
424	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(kv);
425	        lpt->lp = lpDeleteRange(lpt->lp, 0, expired * 3);
426	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
427	            updateSlotAllocSize(db, getKeySlot(key), kv, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(kv));
428	
429	        /* update keysizes */
430	        unsigned long l = lpLength(lpt->lp) / 3;
431	        updateKeysizesHist(db, OBJ_HASH, l + expired, l);
432	    }
433	
434	    min = hashTypeGetMinExpire(kv, 1 /*accurate*/);
435	    info->nextExpireTime = min;
436	}
437	
438	static void listpackExAddInternal(robj *o, listpackEntry ent[3]) {
439	    listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
440	
441	    /* Shortcut, just append at the end if this is a non-volatile field. */
442	    if (ent[2].lval == HASH_LP_NO_TTL) {
443	        lpt->lp = lpBatchAppend(lpt->lp, ent, 3);
444	        return;
445	    }
446	
447	    struct lpFingArgs r = {
448	            .max_to_search = UINT64_MAX,
449	            .expire_time = ent[2].lval,
450	    };
451	
452	    /* Check if there is a field with a larger TTL. */
453	    lpFindCb(lpt->lp, NULL, &r, cbFindInListpack, 0);
454	
455	    /* If list is empty or there is no field with a larger TTL, result will be
456	     * NULL. Otherwise, just insert before the found item.*/
457	    if (r.p)
458	        lpt->lp = lpBatchInsert(lpt->lp, r.p, LP_BEFORE, ent, 3, NULL);
459	    else
460	        lpt->lp = lpBatchAppend(lpt->lp, ent, 3);
461	}
462	
463	/* Add new field ordered by expire time. */
464	void listpackExAddNew(robj *o, char *field, size_t flen,
465	                      char *value, size_t vlen, uint64_t expireAt) {
466	    listpackEntry ent[3] = {
467	        {.sval = (unsigned char*) field, .slen = flen},
468	        {.sval = (unsigned char*) value, .slen = vlen},
469	        {.lval = expireAt}
470	    };
471	
472	    listpackExAddInternal(o, ent);
473	}
474	
475	/* If expiry time is changed, this function will place field into the correct
476	 * position. First, it deletes the field and re-inserts to the listpack ordered
477	 * by expiry time. */
478	static void listpackExUpdateExpiry(robj *o, sds field,
479	                                   unsigned char *fptr,
480	                                   unsigned char *vptr,
481	                                   uint64_t expire_at) {
482	    unsigned int slen = 0;
483	    long long val = 0;
484	    unsigned char tmp[512] = {0};
485	    unsigned char *valstr;
486	    sds tmpval = NULL;
487	    listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
488	
489	    /* Copy value */
490	    valstr = lpGetValue(vptr, &slen, &val);
491	    if (valstr) {
492	        /* Normally, item length in the listpack is limited by
493	         * 'hash-max-listpack-value' config. It is unlikely, but it might be
494	         * larger than sizeof(tmp). */
495	        if (slen > sizeof(tmp))
496	            tmpval = sdsnewlen(valstr, slen);
497	        else
498	            memcpy(tmp, valstr, slen);
499	    }
500	
501	    /* Delete field name, value and expiry time */
502	    lpt->lp = lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(lpt->lp, &fptr, 3);
503	
504	    listpackEntry ent[3] = {{0}};
505	
506	    ent[0].sval = (unsigned char*) field;
507	    ent[0].slen = sdslen(field);
508	
509	    if (valstr) {
510	        ent[1].sval = tmpval ? (unsigned char *) tmpval : tmp;
511	        ent[1].slen = slen;
512	    } else {
513	        ent[1].lval = val;
514	    }
515	    ent[2].lval = expire_at;
516	
517	    listpackExAddInternal(o, ent);
518	    sdsfree(tmpval);
519	}
520	
521	/* Update field expire time. */
522	SetExRes hashTypeSetExpiryListpack(HashTypeSetEx *ex, sds field,
523	                                   unsigned char *fptr, unsigned char *vptr,
524	                                   unsigned char *tptr, uint64_t expireAt)
525	{
526	    long long expireTime;
527	    uint64_t prevExpire = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
528	
529	    serverAssert(lpGetIntegerValue(tptr, &expireTime));
530	
531	    if (expireTime != HASH_LP_NO_TTL) {
532	        prevExpire = (uint64_t) expireTime;
533	    }
534	
535	    /* Special value of EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID indicates field should be persisted.*/
536	    if (expireAt == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
537	        /* Return error if already there is no ttl. */
538	        if (prevExpire == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID)
539	            return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
540	        listpackExUpdateExpiry(ex->hashObj, field, fptr, vptr, HASH_LP_NO_TTL);
541	        return HSETEX_OK;
542	    }
543	
544	    if (prevExpire == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
545	        /* For fields without expiry, LT condition is considered valid */
546	        if (ex->expireSetCond & (HFE_XX | HFE_GT))
547	            return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
548	    } else {
549	        if (((ex->expireSetCond == HFE_GT) && (prevExpire >= expireAt)) ||
550	            ((ex->expireSetCond == HFE_LT) && (prevExpire <= expireAt)) ||
551	            (ex->expireSetCond == HFE_NX) )
552	            return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
553	
554	        /* Track of minimum expiration time (only later update global HFE DS) */
555	        if (ex->minExpireFields > prevExpire)
556	            ex->minExpireFields = prevExpire;
557	    }
558	
559	    /* If expired, then delete the field and propagate the deletion.
560	     * If replica, continue like the field is valid */
561	    if (unlikely(checkAlreadyExpired(expireAt))) {
562	        propagateHashFieldDeletion(ex->db, ex->key->ptr, field, sdslen(field));
563	        hashTypeDelete(ex->hashObj, field);
564	        server.stat_expired_subkeys++;
565	        return HSETEX_DELETED;
566	    }
567	
568	    if (ex->minExpireFields > expireAt)
569	        ex->minExpireFields = expireAt;
570	
571	    listpackExUpdateExpiry(ex->hashObj, field, fptr, vptr, expireAt);
572	    return HSETEX_OK;
573	}
574	
575	/* Returns 1 if expired */
576	int hashTypeIsExpired(const robj *o, uint64_t expireAt) {
577	    if (server.allow_access_expired) return 0;
578	
579	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
580	        if (expireAt == HASH_LP_NO_TTL)
581	            return 0;
582	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
583	        if (expireAt == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID)
584	            return 0;
585	    } else {
586	        serverPanic("Unknown encoding: %d", o->encoding);
587	    }
588	
589	    return (mstime_t) expireAt < commandTimeSnapshot();
590	}
591	
592	/* Returns listpack pointer of the object. */
593	unsigned char *hashTypeListpackGetLp(robj *o) {
594	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK)
595	        return o->ptr;
596	    else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX)
597	        return ((listpackEx*)o->ptr)->lp;
598	
599	    serverPanic("Unknown encoding: %d", o->encoding);
600	}
601	
602	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
603	 * Hash type API
604	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
605	
606	/* Check the length of a number of objects to see if we need to convert a
607	 * listpack to a real hash. Note that we only check string encoded objects
608	 * as their string length can be queried in constant time. */
609	void hashTypeTryConversion(redisDb *db, kvobj *o, robj **argv, int start, int end) {
610	    int i;
611	    size_t sum = 0;
612	
613	    if (o->encoding != OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK && o->encoding != OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX)
614	        return;
615	
616	    /* We guess that most of the values in the input are unique, so
617	     * if there are enough arguments we create a pre-sized hash, which
618	     * might over allocate memory if there are duplicates. */
619	    size_t new_fields = (end - start + 1) / 2;
620	    if (new_fields > server.hash_max_listpack_entries) {
621	        hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
622	        dictExpand(o->ptr, new_fields);
623	        return;
624	    }
625	
626	    for (i = start; i <= end; i++) {
627	        if (!sdsEncodedObject(argv[i]))
628	            continue;
629	        size_t len = sdslen(argv[i]->ptr);
630	        if (len > server.hash_max_listpack_value) {
631	            hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
632	            return;
633	        }
634	        sum += len;
635	    }
636	    if (!lpSafeToAdd(hashTypeListpackGetLp(o), sum)) {
637	        hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
638	    }
639	}
640	
641	/* Get the value from a listpack encoded hash, identified by field. */
642	GetFieldRes hashTypeGetFromListpack(robj *o, sds field,
643	                            unsigned char **vstr,
644	                            unsigned int *vlen,
645	                            long long *vll,
646	                            uint64_t *expiredAt)
647	{
648	    *expiredAt = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
649	    unsigned char *zl, *fptr = NULL, *vptr = NULL;
650	
651	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
652	        zl = o->ptr;
653	        fptr = lpFirst(zl);
654	        if (fptr != NULL) {
655	            fptr = lpFind(zl, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, sdslen(field), 1);
656	            if (fptr != NULL) {
657	                /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
658	                vptr = lpNext(zl, fptr);
659	                serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
660	            }
661	        }
662	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
663	        long long expire;
664	        unsigned char *h;
665	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
666	
667	        fptr = lpFirst(lpt->lp);
668	        if (fptr != NULL) {
669	            fptr = lpFind(lpt->lp, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, sdslen(field), 2);
670	            if (fptr != NULL) {
671	                vptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, fptr);
672	                serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
673	
674	                h = lpNext(lpt->lp, vptr);
675	                serverAssert(h && lpGetIntegerValue(h, &expire));
676	                if (expire != HASH_LP_NO_TTL)
677	                    *expiredAt = expire;
678	            }
679	        }
680	    } else {
681	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding: %d", o->encoding);
682	    }
683	
684	    if (vptr != NULL) {
685	        *vstr = lpGetValue(vptr, vlen, vll);
686	        return GETF_OK;
687	    }
688	
689	    return GETF_NOT_FOUND;
690	}
691	
692	/* Get the value from a hash table encoded hash, identified by field.
693	 * Returns NULL when the field cannot be found, otherwise the SDS value
694	 * is returned. */
695	GetFieldRes hashTypeGetFromHashTable(robj *o, sds field, sds *value, uint64_t *expiredAt) {
696	    dictEntry *de;
697	
698	    *expiredAt = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
699	
700	    serverAssert(o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
701	
702	    de = dictFind(o->ptr, field);
703	
704	    if (de == NULL)
705	        return GETF_NOT_FOUND;
706	
707	    Entry *entry = dictGetKey(de);
708	    *expiredAt = entryGetExpiry(entry);
709	    *value = entryGetValue(entry);
710	    return GETF_OK;
711	}
712	
713	/* Higher level function of hashTypeGet*() that returns the hash value
714	 * associated with the specified field.
715	 * Arguments:
716	 * hfeFlags      - Lookup for HFE_LAZY_* flags
717	 *
718	 * Returned:
719	 * GetFieldRes  - Result of get operation
720	 * vstr, vlen   - if string, ref in either *vstr and *vlen if it's
721	 *                returned in string form,
722	 * vll          - or stored in *vll if it's returned as a number.
723	 *                If *vll is populated *vstr is set to NULL, so the caller can
724	 *                always check the function return by checking the return value
725	 *                for GETF_OK and checking if vll (or vstr) is NULL.
726	 * expiredAt    - if the field has an expiration time, it will be set to the expiration 
727	 *                time of the field. Otherwise, will be set to EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID.
728	 */
729	GetFieldRes hashTypeGetValue(redisDb *db, kvobj *o, sds field, unsigned char **vstr,
730	                                   unsigned int *vlen, long long *vll, 
731	                                   int hfeFlags, uint64_t *expiredAt)
732	{
733	    sds key = kvobjGetKey(o);
734	    GetFieldRes res;
735	    uint64_t dummy;
736	    size_t oldsize = 0;
737	    if (expiredAt == NULL) expiredAt = &dummy;
738	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK ||
739	        o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
740	        *vstr = NULL;
741	        res = hashTypeGetFromListpack(o, field, vstr, vlen, vll, expiredAt);
742	
743	        if (res == GETF_NOT_FOUND)
744	            return GETF_NOT_FOUND;
745	
746	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
747	        sds value = NULL;
748	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled && !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_ALLOCSIZES))
749	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(o);
750	        res = hashTypeGetFromHashTable(o, field, &value, expiredAt);
751	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled && !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_ALLOCSIZES))
752	            updateSlotAllocSize(db, getKeySlot(key), o, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(o));
753	
754	        if (res == GETF_NOT_FOUND)
755	            return GETF_NOT_FOUND;
756	
757	        *vstr = (unsigned char*) value;
758	        *vlen = sdslen(value);
759	    } else {
760	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
761	    }
762	
763	    if ((server.allow_access_expired) ||
764	        (*expiredAt >= (uint64_t) commandTimeSnapshot()) ||
765	        (hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_ACCESS_EXPIRED))
766	        return GETF_OK;
767	
768	    if (server.masterhost || server.cluster_enabled) {
769	        /* If CLIENT_MASTER, assume valid as long as it didn't get delete.
770	         *
771	         * In cluster mode, we also assume valid if we are importing data
772	         * from the source, to avoid deleting fields that are still in use.
773	         * We create a fake master client for data import, which can be
774	         * identified using the CLIENT_MASTER flag. */
775	        if (server.current_client && (server.current_client->flags & CLIENT_MASTER))
776	            return GETF_OK;
777	
778	        /* For replica, if user client, then act as if expired, but don't delete! */
779	        if (server.masterhost) return GETF_EXPIRED;
780	    }
781	
782	    if ((server.loading) ||
783	        (hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_AVOID_FIELD_DEL) ||
784	        (isPausedActionsWithUpdate(PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE)))
785	        return GETF_EXPIRED;
786	
787	    /* delete the field and propagate the deletion */
788	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled && !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_ALLOCSIZES))
789	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(o);
790	    serverAssert(hashTypeDelete(o, field) == 1);
791	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled && !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_ALLOCSIZES))
792	        updateSlotAllocSize(db, getKeySlot(key), o, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(o));
793	    propagateHashFieldDeletion(db, key, field, sdslen(field));
794	    server.stat_expired_subkeys++;
795	
796	    if (!(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_KEYSIZES)) {
797	        uint64_t l = hashTypeLength(o, 0);
798	        updateKeysizesHist(db, OBJ_HASH, l+1, l);
799	    }
800	
801	    /* If the field is the last one in the hash, then the hash will be deleted */
802	    res = GETF_EXPIRED;
803	    robj *keyObj = createStringObject(key, sdslen(key));
804	    unsigned long length = hashTypeLength(o, 0);
805	    if ((length != 0) && !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_NOTIFICATION)) {
806	        robj fobj, *farr[1] = {&fobj};
807	        initStaticStringObject(fobj, field);
808	        notifyKeyspaceEventWithSubkeys(NOTIFY_HASH, "hexpired", keyObj, db->id, farr, 1);
809	    }
810	    if ((length == 0) && (!(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_AVOID_HASH_DEL))) {
811	        if (!(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_NOTIFICATION))
812	            notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC, "del", keyObj, db->id);
813	        dbDelete(db,keyObj);
814	        o = NULL;
815	        res = GETF_EXPIRED_HASH;
816	    }
817	    keyModified(NULL, db, keyObj, o, !(hfeFlags & HFE_LAZY_NO_SIGNAL));
818	    decrRefCount(keyObj);
819	    return res;
820	}
821	
822	/* Like hashTypeGetValue() but returns a Redis object, which is useful for
823	 * interaction with the hash type outside t_hash.c.
824	 * The function returns NULL if the field is not found in the hash. Otherwise
825	 * a newly allocated string object with the value is returned.
826	 *
827	 * hfeFlags      - Lookup HFE_LAZY_* flags
828	 * isHashDeleted - If attempted to access expired field and it's the last field
829	 *                 in the hash, then the hash will as well be deleted. In this case,
830	 *                 isHashDeleted will be set to 1.
831	 * val           - If the field is found, then val will be set to the value object.
832	 * expireTime    - If the field exists (`GETF_OK`) then expireTime will be set to  
833	 *                 the expiration time of the field. Otherwise, it will be set to 0.
834	 *                 
835	 * Returns 1 if the field exists, and 0 when it doesn't.
836	 */
837	int hashTypeGetValueObject(redisDb *db, kvobj *o, sds field, int hfeFlags,
838	                           robj **val, uint64_t *expireTime, int *isHashDeleted) {
839	    unsigned char *vstr;
840	    unsigned int vlen;
841	    long long vll;
842	
843	    if (isHashDeleted) *isHashDeleted = 0;
844	    if (val) *val = NULL;
845	    GetFieldRes res = hashTypeGetValue(db,o,field,&vstr,&vlen,&vll, 
846	                                                   hfeFlags, expireTime);
847	
848	    if (res == GETF_OK) {
849	        /* expireTime set to 0 if the field has no expiration time */ 
850	        if (expireTime && (*expireTime == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID))
851	            *expireTime = 0;
852	        
853	        /* If expected to return the value, then create a new object */
854	        if (val) {
855	            if (vstr) *val = createStringObject((char *) vstr, vlen);
856	            else *val = createStringObjectFromLongLong(vll);
857	        }
858	        return 1;
859	    }
860	
861	    if ((res == GETF_EXPIRED_HASH) && (isHashDeleted))
862	        *isHashDeleted = 1;
863	
864	    /* GETF_EXPIRED_HASH, GETF_EXPIRED, GETF_NOT_FOUND */
865	    return 0;
866	}
867	
868	/* Test if the specified field exists in the given hash. If the field is
869	 * expired (HFE), then it will be lazy deleted unless HFE_LAZY_AVOID_FIELD_DEL 
870	 * hfeFlags is set.
871	 *
872	 * hfeFlags      - Lookup HFE_LAZY_* flags
873	 * isHashDeleted - If attempted to access expired field and it is the last field
874	 *                 in the hash, then the hash will as well be deleted. In this case,
875	 *                 isHashDeleted will be set to 1.
876	 *
877	 * Returns 1 if the field exists, and 0 when it doesn't.
878	 */
879	int hashTypeExists(redisDb *db, kvobj *o, sds field, int hfeFlags, int *isHashDeleted) {
880	    unsigned char *vstr = NULL;
881	    unsigned int vlen = UINT_MAX;
882	    long long vll = LLONG_MAX;
883	
884	    GetFieldRes res = hashTypeGetValue(db, o, field, &vstr, &vlen, &vll, 
885	                                             hfeFlags, NULL);
886	    if (isHashDeleted)
887	        *isHashDeleted = (res == GETF_EXPIRED_HASH) ? 1 : 0;
888	    return (res == GETF_OK) ? 1 : 0;
889	}
890	
891	/* Add a new field, overwrite the old with the new value if it already exists.
892	 * Return 0 on insert and 1 on update.
893	 *
894	 * By default, the key and value SDS strings are copied if needed, so the
895	 * caller retains ownership of the strings passed. However this behavior
896	 * can be effected by passing appropriate flags (possibly bitwise OR-ed):
897	 *
898	 * HASH_SET_TAKE_FIELD  -- The SDS field ownership passes to the function.
899	 * HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE  -- The SDS value ownership passes to the function.
900	 * HASH_SET_KEEP_TTL --  keep original TTL if field already exists
901	 *
902	 * When the flags are used the caller does not need to release the passed
903	 * SDS string(s). It's up to the function to use the string to create a new
904	 * entry or to free the SDS string before returning to the caller.
905	 *
906	 * HASH_SET_COPY corresponds to no flags passed, and means the default
907	 * semantics of copying the values if needed.
908	 *
909	 */
910	#define HASH_SET_TAKE_FIELD  (1<<0)
911	#define HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE  (1<<1)
912	#define HASH_SET_KEEP_TTL (1<<2)
913	
914	static_assert(HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE == ENTRY_TAKE_VALUE, "ENTRY_TAKE_VALUE must match HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE");
915	
916	int hashTypeSet(redisDb *db, kvobj *o, sds field, sds value, int flags) {
917	    int update = 0;
918	
919	    /* Check if the field is too long for listpack, and convert before adding the item.
920	     * This is needed for HINCRBY* case since in other commands this is handled early by
921	     * hashTypeTryConversion, so this check will be a NOP. */
922	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK  ||
923	        o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
924	        if (sdslen(field) > server.hash_max_listpack_value || sdslen(value) > server.hash_max_listpack_value)
925	            hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
926	    }
927	
928	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
929	        unsigned char *zl, *fptr, *vptr;
930	
931	        zl = o->ptr;
932	        fptr = lpFirst(zl);
933	        if (fptr != NULL) {
934	            fptr = lpFind(zl, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, sdslen(field), 1);
935	            if (fptr != NULL) {
936	                /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
937	                vptr = lpNext(zl, fptr);
938	                serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
939	
940	                /* Replace value */
941	                zl = lpReplace(zl, &vptr, (unsigned char*)value, sdslen(value));
942	                update = 1;
943	            }
944	        }
945	
946	        if (!update) {
947	            listpackEntry entries[2] = {
948	                {.sval = (unsigned char*) field, .slen = sdslen(field)},
949	                {.sval = (unsigned char*) value, .slen = sdslen(value)},
950	            };
951	
952	            /* Push new field/value pair onto the tail of the listpack */
953	            zl = lpBatchAppend(zl, entries, 2);
954	        }
955	        o->ptr = zl;
956	
957	        /* Check if the listpack needs to be converted to a hash table */
958	        if (hashTypeLength(o, 0) > server.hash_max_listpack_entries)
959	            hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
960	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
961	        unsigned char *fptr = NULL, *vptr = NULL, *tptr = NULL;
962	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
963	        long long expireTime = HASH_LP_NO_TTL;
964	
965	        fptr = lpFirst(lpt->lp);
966	        if (fptr != NULL) {
967	            fptr = lpFind(lpt->lp, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, sdslen(field), 2);
968	            if (fptr != NULL) {
969	                /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
970	                vptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, fptr);
971	                serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
972	
973	                /* Replace value */
974	                lpt->lp = lpReplace(lpt->lp, &vptr, (unsigned char *) value, sdslen(value));
975	                update = 1;
976	
977	                fptr = lpPrev(lpt->lp, vptr);
978	                serverAssert(fptr != NULL);
979	
980	                tptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, vptr);
981	                serverAssert(tptr && lpGetIntegerValue(tptr, &expireTime));
982	
983	                if (flags & HASH_SET_KEEP_TTL) {
984	                    /* keep old field along with TTL */
985	                } else if (expireTime != HASH_LP_NO_TTL) {
986	                    /* re-insert field and override TTL */
987	                    listpackExUpdateExpiry(o, field, fptr, vptr, HASH_LP_NO_TTL);
988	                }
989	            }
990	        }
991	
992	        if (!update)
993	            listpackExAddNew(o, field, sdslen(field), value, sdslen(value),
994	                             HASH_LP_NO_TTL);
995	
996	        /* Check if the listpack needs to be converted to a hash table */
997	        if (hashTypeLength(o, 0) > server.hash_max_listpack_entries)
998	            hashTypeConvert(db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
999	
1000	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1001	        dict *ht = o->ptr;
1002	        /* check if field already exists */
1003	        dictEntryLink bucket, link = dictFindLink(ht, field, &bucket);
1004	        size_t *alloc_size = htGetMetadataSize(ht);
1005	
1006	        /* take ownership of value if requested */
1007	        uint32_t newEntryFlags = flags & HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE;
1008	        flags &= ~HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE;
1009	
1010	        if (link == NULL) {
1011	            /* Create entry and transfer value ownership if possible */
1012	            size_t usable;
1013	            Entry *newEntry = entryCreate(field, value, newEntryFlags, &usable);
1014	
1015	            dictSetKeyAtLink(ht, newEntry, &bucket, 1);
1016	            *alloc_size += usable;
1017	        } else {
1018	            /* Existing field - update value in entry */
1019	            Entry *oldEntry = dictGetKey(*link);
1020	
1021	            /* Check if old entry has expiration before potentially freeing it */
1022	            uint64_t oldExpireAt = entryGetExpiry(oldEntry);
1023	            uint64_t newExpireAt = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1024	
1025	            /* If attached TTL to the old field, then remove it from hash's
1026	             * private ebuckets. We do this before updating the value because
1027	             * the entry might be reallocated and freed. */
1028	            if (oldExpireAt != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
1029	                hfieldPersist(o, oldEntry);
1030	                if (flags & HASH_SET_KEEP_TTL) {
1031	                    newExpireAt = oldExpireAt;
1032	                    newEntryFlags |= ENTRY_HAS_EXPIRY;
1033	                }
1034	            }
1035	            
1036	            ssize_t usableDiff;
1037	            Entry *newEntry = entryUpdate(oldEntry, value, newEntryFlags, &usableDiff);
1038	
1039	            /* If entry was reallocated, update the dict key */
1040	            if (newEntry != oldEntry) {
1041	                /* entryUpdate already freed the old entry if needed */
1042	                /* Update the dict to point to the new entry using dictSetKeyAtLink (no_value=1) */
1043	                dictSetKeyAtLink(ht, newEntry, &link, 0);
1044	            }
1045	
1046	            /* If keeping TTL, add the (potentially new) entry back to ebuckets */
1047	            if (newExpireAt != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
1048	                dict *d = o->ptr;
1049	                htMetadataEx *dictExpireMeta = htGetMetadataEx(d);
1050	                ebAdd(&dictExpireMeta->hfe, &hashFieldExpireBucketsType, newEntry, newExpireAt);
1051	            }
1052	
1053	            *alloc_size += usableDiff;
1054	            update = 1;
1055	        }
1056	    } else {
1057	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1058	    }
1059	
1060	    /* Free SDS strings we did not referenced elsewhere if the flags
1061	     * want this function to be responsible. */
1062	    if (flags & HASH_SET_TAKE_FIELD && field) sdsfree(field);
1063	    if (flags & HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE && value) sdsfree(value);
1064	    return update;
1065	}
1066	
1067	SetExRes hashTypeSetExpiryHT(HashTypeSetEx *exInfo, sds field, uint64_t expireAt) {
1068	    dict *ht = exInfo->hashObj->ptr;
1069	    dictEntryLink link = NULL;
1070	    Entry *entryNew = NULL;
1071	
1072	    link = dictFindLink(ht, field, NULL);
1073	    if (link == NULL)
1074	        return HSETEX_NO_FIELD;
1075	
1076	    dictEntry *existingEntry = *link;
1077	    Entry *oldEntry = dictGetKey(existingEntry);
1078	    /* Special value of EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID indicates field should be persisted.*/
1079	    if (expireAt == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
1080	        /* Return error if already there is no ttl. */
1081	        if (entryGetExpiry(oldEntry) == EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID)
1082	            return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
1083	
1084	        hfieldPersist(exInfo->hashObj, oldEntry);
1085	        return HSETEX_OK;
1086	    }
1087	
1088	    /* If field doesn't have expiry metadata attached */
1089	    if (!entryHasExpiry(oldEntry)) {
1090	        size_t *alloc_size = htGetMetadataSize(ht);
1091	
1092	        /* For fields without expiry, LT condition is considered valid */
1093	        if (exInfo->expireSetCond & (HFE_XX | HFE_GT))
1094	            return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
1095	
1096	        ssize_t usableDiff;
1097	        entryNew = entryUpdate(oldEntry, NULL, ENTRY_HAS_EXPIRY, &usableDiff);
1098	        *alloc_size += usableDiff;
1099	    } else { /* field has ExpireMeta struct attached */
1100	        uint64_t prevExpire = entryGetExpiry(oldEntry);
1101	
1102	        /* If field has valid expiration time, then check GT|LT|NX */
1103	        if (prevExpire != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
1104	            if (((exInfo->expireSetCond == HFE_GT) && (prevExpire >= expireAt)) ||
1105	                ((exInfo->expireSetCond == HFE_LT) && (prevExpire <= expireAt)) ||
1106	                (exInfo->expireSetCond == HFE_NX) )
1107	                return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
1108	
1109	            /* If expiry time is the same, then nothing to do */
1110	            if (prevExpire == expireAt)
1111	                return HSETEX_OK;
1112	
1113	            /* remove old expiry time from hash's private ebuckets */
1114	            htMetadataEx *dm = htGetMetadataEx(ht);
1115	            ebRemove(&dm->hfe, &hashFieldExpireBucketsType, oldEntry);
1116	
1117	            /* Track of minimum expiration time (only later update global HFE DS) */
1118	            if (exInfo->minExpireFields > prevExpire)
1119	                exInfo->minExpireFields = prevExpire;
1120	
1121	        } else {
1122	            /* field has invalid expiry. No need to ebRemove() */
1123	
1124	            /* Check XX|LT|GT */
1125	            if (exInfo->expireSetCond & (HFE_XX | HFE_GT))
1126	                return HSETEX_NO_CONDITION_MET;
1127	        }
1128	
1129	        /* Reuse hfOld as hfNew and rewrite its expiry with ebAdd() */
1130	        entryNew = oldEntry;
1131	    }
1132	
1133	    dictSetKeyAtLink(ht, entryNew, &link, 0);  /* newItem=0 for updating existing entry */
1134	
1135	
1136	    /* If expired, then delete the field and propagate the deletion.
1137	     * If replica, continue like the field is valid */
1138	    if (unlikely(checkAlreadyExpired(expireAt))) {
1139	        /* replicas should not initiate deletion of fields */
1140	        propagateHashFieldDeletion(exInfo->db, exInfo->key->ptr, field, sdslen(field));
1141	        hashTypeDelete(exInfo->hashObj, field);
1142	        server.stat_expired_subkeys++;
1143	        return HSETEX_DELETED;
1144	    }
1145	
1146	    if (exInfo->minExpireFields > expireAt)
1147	        exInfo->minExpireFields = expireAt;
1148	
1149	    htMetadataEx *dm = htGetMetadataEx(ht);
1150	    ebAdd(&dm->hfe, &hashFieldExpireBucketsType, entryNew, expireAt);
1151	    return HSETEX_OK;
1152	}
1153	
1154	/*
1155	 * Set field expiration
1156	 *
1157	 * Take care to call first hashTypeSetExInit() and then call this function.
1158	 * Finally, call hashTypeSetExDone() to notify and update global HFE DS.
1159	 *
1160	 * Special value of EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID for 'expireAt' argument will persist
1161	 * the field.
1162	 */
1163	SetExRes hashTypeSetEx(robj *o, sds field, uint64_t expireAt, HashTypeSetEx *exInfo) {
1164	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1165	        unsigned char *fptr = NULL, *vptr = NULL, *tptr = NULL;
1166	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
1167	
1168	        fptr = lpFirst(lpt->lp);
1169	        if (fptr)
1170	            fptr = lpFind(lpt->lp, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, sdslen(field), 2);
1171	
1172	        if (!fptr)
1173	            return HSETEX_NO_FIELD;
1174	
1175	        /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
1176	        vptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, fptr);
1177	        serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
1178	
1179	        tptr = lpNext(lpt->lp, vptr);
1180	        serverAssert(tptr);
1181	
1182	        /* update TTL */
1183	        return hashTypeSetExpiryListpack(exInfo, field, fptr, vptr, tptr, expireAt);
1184	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1185	        /* If needed to set the field along with expiry */
1186	        return hashTypeSetExpiryHT(exInfo, field, expireAt);
1187	    } else {
1188	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1189	    }
1190	
1191	    return HSETEX_OK; /* never reach here */
1192	}
1193	
1194	void initDictExpireMetadata(robj *o) {
1195	    dict *ht = o->ptr;
1196	
1197	    htMetadataEx *m = htGetMetadataEx(ht);
1198	    m->hfe = ebCreate();     /* Allocate HFE DS */
1199	    m->expireMeta.trash = 1; /* mark as trash (as long it wasn't ebAdd()) */
1200	}
1201	
1202	/* Init HashTypeSetEx struct before calling hashTypeSetEx() */
1203	int hashTypeSetExInit(robj *key, kvobj *o, client *c, redisDb *db,
1204	                      ExpireSetCond expireSetCond, HashTypeSetEx *ex)
1205	{
1206	    dict *ht = o->ptr;
1207	    ex->expireSetCond = expireSetCond;
1208	    ex->minExpire = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1209	    ex->c = c;
1210	    ex->db = db;
1211	    ex->key = key;
1212	    ex->hashObj = o;
1213	    ex->minExpireFields = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1214	
1215	    /* Take care that HASH support expiration */
1216	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1217	        hashTypeConvert(c->db, o, OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX);
1218	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1219	        /* Take care dict has HFE metadata */
1220	        if (!isDictWithMetaHFE(ht)) {
1221	            /* Realloc (only header of dict) with metadata for hash-field expiration */
1222	            dictTypeAddMeta(&ht, &entryHashDictTypeWithHFE);
1223	            htMetadataEx *m = htGetMetadataEx(ht);
1224	            o->ptr = ht;
1225	
1226	            /* Find the key in the keyspace. Need to keep reference to the key for
1227	             * notifications or even removal of the hash */
1228	
1229	            /* Fillup dict HFE metadata */
1230	            m->hfe = ebCreate();     /* Allocate HFE DS */
1231	            m->expireMeta.trash = 1; /* mark as trash (as long it wasn't ebAdd()) */
1232	        }
1233	    }
1234	
1235	    /* Read minExpire from attached ExpireMeta to the hash */
1236	    ex->minExpire = hashTypeGetMinExpire(o, 0);
1237	    return C_OK;
1238	}
1239	
1240	/*
1241	 * After calling hashTypeSetEx() for setting fields or their expiry, call this
1242	 * function to update global HFE DS.
1243	 */
1244	void hashTypeSetExDone(HashTypeSetEx *ex) {
1245	
1246	    if (hashTypeLength(ex->hashObj, 0) == 0)
1247	        return;
1248	
1249	    /* If minimum HFE of the hash is smaller than expiration time of the
1250	     * specified fields in the command as well as it is smaller or equal
1251	     * than expiration time provided in the command, then the minimum
1252	     * HFE of the hash won't change following this command. */
1253	    if ((ex->minExpire < ex->minExpireFields))
1254	        return;
1255	
1256	    /* Retrieve new expired time. It might have changed. */
1257	    uint64_t newMinExpire = hashTypeGetMinExpire(ex->hashObj, 1 /*accurate*/);
1258	
1259	    /* Calculate the diff between old minExpire and newMinExpire. If it is
1260	     * only few seconds, then don't have to update global HFE DS. At the worst
1261	     * case fields of hash will be active-expired up to few seconds later.
1262	     *
1263	     * In any case, active-expire operation will know to update global
1264	     * HFE DS more efficiently than here for a single item.
1265	     */
1266	    uint64_t diff = (ex->minExpire > newMinExpire) ?
1267	                    (ex->minExpire - newMinExpire) : (newMinExpire - ex->minExpire);
1268	    if (diff < HASH_NEW_EXPIRE_DIFF_THRESHOLD) return;
1269	
1270	    int slot = getKeySlot(ex->key->ptr);
1271	    if (ex->minExpire != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID) {
1272	        if (newMinExpire != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID)
1273	            estoreUpdate(ex->db->subexpires, slot, ex->hashObj, newMinExpire);
1274	        else
1275	            estoreRemove(ex->db->subexpires, slot, ex->hashObj);
1276	    } else {
1277	        if (newMinExpire != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID)
1278	            estoreAdd(ex->db->subexpires, slot, ex->hashObj, newMinExpire);
1279	    }
1280	}
1281	
1282	/* Delete an element from a hash.
1283	 *
1284	 * Return 1 on deleted and 0 on not found.
1285	 * field - sds field name to delete */
1286	int hashTypeDelete(robj *o, void *field) {
1287	    int deleted = 0;
1288	    int fieldLen = sdslen((sds)field);
1289	
1290	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1291	        unsigned char *zl, *fptr;
1292	
1293	        zl = o->ptr;
1294	        fptr = lpFirst(zl);
1295	        if (fptr != NULL) {
1296	            fptr = lpFind(zl, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, fieldLen, 1);
1297	            if (fptr != NULL) {
1298	                /* Delete both of the key and the value. */
1299	                zl = lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(zl,&fptr,2);
1300	                o->ptr = zl;
1301	                deleted = 1;
1302	            }
1303	        }
1304	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1305	        unsigned char *fptr;
1306	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
1307	
1308	        fptr = lpFirst(lpt->lp);
1309	        if (fptr != NULL) {
1310	            fptr = lpFind(lpt->lp, fptr, (unsigned char*)field, fieldLen, 2);
1311	            if (fptr != NULL) {
1312	                /* Delete field, value and ttl */
1313	                lpt->lp = lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(lpt->lp, &fptr, 3);
1314	                deleted = 1;
1315	            }
1316	        }
1317	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1318	        /* dictDelete() will call dictEntryDestructor() */
1319	        if (dictDelete((dict*)o->ptr, field) == C_OK) {
1320	            deleted = 1;
1321	        }
1322	    } else {
1323	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1324	    }
1325	    return deleted;
1326	}
1327	
1328	/* Return the number of elements in a hash.
1329	 *
1330	 * Note, subtractExpiredFields=1 might be pricy in case there are many HFEs
1331	 */
1332	unsigned long hashTypeLength(const robj *o, int subtractExpiredFields) {
1333	    unsigned long length = ULONG_MAX;
1334	    /* If expired field access is allowed, don't subtract expired fields from the count. */
1335	    if (server.allow_access_expired)
1336	        subtractExpiredFields = 0;
1337	
1338	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1339	        length = lpLength(o->ptr) / 2;
1340	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1341	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
1342	        length = lpLength(lpt->lp) / 3;
1343	
1344	        if (subtractExpiredFields && lpt->meta.trash == 0)
1345	            length -= listpackExExpireDryRun(o);
1346	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1347	        uint64_t expiredItems = 0;
1348	        dict *d = (dict*)o->ptr;
1349	        if (subtractExpiredFields && isDictWithMetaHFE(d)) {
1350	            htMetadataEx *meta = htGetMetadataEx(d);
1351	            /* If dict registered in global HFE DS */
1352	            if (meta->expireMeta.trash == 0)
1353	                expiredItems = ebExpireDryRun(meta->hfe,
1354	                                              &hashFieldExpireBucketsType,
1355	                                              commandTimeSnapshot());
1356	        }
1357	        length = dictSize(d) - expiredItems;
1358	    } else {
1359	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1360	    }
1361	    return length;
1362	}
1363	
1364	size_t hashTypeAllocSize(const robj *o) {
1365	    serverAssertWithInfo(NULL,o,o->type == OBJ_HASH);
1366	    size_t size = 0;
1367	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1368	        size = lpBytes(o->ptr);
1369	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1370	        listpackEx *lpt = o->ptr;
1371	        size = sizeof(listpackEx) + lpBytes(lpt->lp);
1372	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1373	        dict *d = o->ptr;
1374	        size += sizeof(dict) + dictMemUsage(d) + *htGetMetadataSize(d);
1375	    } else {
1376	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1377	    }
1378	    return size;
1379	}

1381	void hashTypeInitIterator(hashTypeIterator *hi, robj *subject) {
1382	    hi->subject = subject;
1383	    hi->encoding = subject->encoding;
1384	
1385	    if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK ||
1386	        hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX)
1387	    {
1388	        hi->fptr = NULL;
1389	        hi->vptr = NULL;
1390	        hi->tptr = NULL;
1391	        hi->expire_time = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1392	    } else if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1393	        dictInitIterator(&hi->di, subject->ptr);
1394	    } else {
1395	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1396	    }
1397	}

1399	void hashTypeResetIterator(hashTypeIterator *hi) {
1400	    if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT)
1401	        dictResetIterator(&hi->di);
1402	}
1403	
1404	/* Move to the next entry in the hash. Return C_OK when the next entry
1405	 * could be found and C_ERR when the iterator reaches the end. */
1406	int hashTypeNext(hashTypeIterator *hi, int skipExpiredFields) {
1407	    /* If expired field access is allowed, don't skip expired fields during iteration */
1408	    if (server.allow_access_expired)
1409	        skipExpiredFields = 0;
1410	
1411	    hi->expire_time = EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1412	    if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1413	        unsigned char *zl;
1414	        unsigned char *fptr, *vptr;
1415	
1416	        zl = hi->subject->ptr;
1417	        fptr = hi->fptr;
1418	        vptr = hi->vptr;
1419	
1420	        if (fptr == NULL) {
1421	            /* Initialize cursor */
1422	            serverAssert(vptr == NULL);
1423	            fptr = lpFirst(zl);
1424	        } else {
1425	            /* Advance cursor */
1426	            serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
1427	            fptr = lpNext(zl, vptr);
1428	        }
1429	        if (fptr == NULL) return C_ERR;
1430	
1431	        /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
1432	        vptr = lpNext(zl, fptr);
1433	        serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
1434	
1435	        /* fptr, vptr now point to the first or next pair */
1436	        hi->fptr = fptr;
1437	        hi->vptr = vptr;
1438	    } else if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1439	        long long expire_time;
1440	        unsigned char *zl = hashTypeListpackGetLp(hi->subject);
1441	        unsigned char *fptr, *vptr, *tptr;
1442	
1443	        fptr = hi->fptr;
1444	        vptr = hi->vptr;
1445	        tptr = hi->tptr;
1446	
1447	        if (fptr == NULL) {
1448	            /* Initialize cursor */
1449	            serverAssert(vptr == NULL);
1450	            fptr = lpFirst(zl);
1451	        } else {
1452	            /* Advance cursor */
1453	            serverAssert(tptr != NULL);
1454	            fptr = lpNext(zl, tptr);
1455	        }
1456	        if (fptr == NULL) return C_ERR;
1457	
1458	        while (fptr != NULL) {
1459	            /* Grab pointer to the value (fptr points to the field) */
1460	            vptr = lpNext(zl, fptr);
1461	            serverAssert(vptr != NULL);
1462	
1463	            tptr = lpNext(zl, vptr);
1464	            serverAssert(tptr && lpGetIntegerValue(tptr, &expire_time));
1465	
1466	            if (!skipExpiredFields || !hashTypeIsExpired(hi->subject, expire_time))
1467	                break;
1468	
1469	            fptr = lpNext(zl, tptr);
1470	        }
1471	        if (fptr == NULL) return C_ERR;
1472	
1473	        /* fptr, vptr now point to the first or next pair */
1474	        hi->fptr = fptr;
1475	        hi->vptr = vptr;
1476	        hi->tptr = tptr;
1477	        hi->expire_time = (expire_time != HASH_LP_NO_TTL) ? (uint64_t) expire_time : EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID;
1478	    } else if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1479	
1480	        while ((hi->de = dictNext(&hi->di)) != NULL) {
1481	            Entry *e = dictGetKey(hi->de);
1482	            hi->expire_time = entryGetExpiry(e);
1483	            /* this condition still valid if expire_time equals EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID */
1484	            if (skipExpiredFields && ((mstime_t)hi->expire_time < commandTimeSnapshot()))
1485	                continue;
1486	            return C_OK;
1487	        }
1488	        return C_ERR;
1489	    } else {
1490	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1491	    }
1492	    return C_OK;
1493	}
1494	
1495	/* Get the field or value at iterator cursor, for an iterator on a hash value
1496	 * encoded as a listpack. Prototype is similar to `hashTypeGetFromListpack`. */
1497	void hashTypeCurrentFromListpack(hashTypeIterator *hi, int what,
1498	                                 unsigned char **vstr,
1499	                                 unsigned int *vlen,
1500	                                 long long *vll,
1501	                                 uint64_t *expireTime)
1502	{
1503	    serverAssert(hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK ||
1504	                 hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX);
1505	
1506	    if (what & OBJ_HASH_KEY) {
1507	        *vstr = lpGetValue(hi->fptr, vlen, vll);
1508	    } else {
1509	        *vstr = lpGetValue(hi->vptr, vlen, vll);
1510	    }
1511	
1512	    if (expireTime)
1513	        *expireTime = hi->expire_time;
1514	}
1515	
1516	/* Get the field or value at iterator cursor, for an iterator on a hash value
1517	 * encoded as a hash table. Prototype is similar to
1518	 * `hashTypeGetFromHashTable`.
1519	 *
1520	 * expireTime - If parameter is not null, then the function will return the expire
1521	 *              time of the field. If expiry not set, return EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID
1522	 */
1523	void hashTypeCurrentFromHashTable(hashTypeIterator *hi, int what, char **str, size_t *len, uint64_t *expireTime) {
1524	    serverAssert(hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
1525	    Entry *e = dictGetKey(hi->de);
1526	
1527	    if (what & OBJ_HASH_KEY) {
1528	        sds field = entryGetField(e);
1529	        *str = field;
1530	        *len = sdslen(field);
1531	    } else {
1532	        sds val = entryGetValue(e);
1533	        *str = val;
1534	        *len = sdslen(val);
1535	    }
1536	
1537	    if (expireTime)
1538	        *expireTime = hi->expire_time;
1539	}
1540	
1541	/* Higher level function of hashTypeCurrent*() that returns the hash value
1542	 * at current iterator position.
1543	 *
1544	 * The returned element is returned by reference in either *vstr and *vlen if
1545	 * it's returned in string form, or stored in *vll if it's returned as
1546	 * a number.
1547	 *
1548	 * If *vll is populated *vstr is set to NULL, so the caller
1549	 * can always check the function return by checking the return value
1550	 * type checking if vstr == NULL. */
1551	void hashTypeCurrentObject(hashTypeIterator *hi,
1552	                           int what,
1553	                           unsigned char **vstr,
1554	                           unsigned int *vlen,
1555	                           long long *vll,
1556	                           uint64_t *expireTime)
1557	{
1558	    if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK ||
1559	        hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX)
1560	    {
1561	        *vstr = NULL;
1562	        hashTypeCurrentFromListpack(hi, what, vstr, vlen, vll, expireTime);
1563	    } else if (hi->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1564	        char *ele;
1565	        size_t eleLen;
1566	        hashTypeCurrentFromHashTable(hi, what, &ele, &eleLen, expireTime);
1567	        *vstr = (unsigned char*) ele;
1568	        *vlen = eleLen;
1569	    } else {
1570	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1571	    }
1572	}
1573	
1574	/* Return the key or value at the current iterator position as a new
1575	 * SDS string. */
1576	sds hashTypeCurrentObjectNewSds(hashTypeIterator *hi, int what) {
1577	    unsigned char *vstr;
1578	    unsigned int vlen;
1579	    long long vll;
1580	
1581	    hashTypeCurrentObject(hi,what,&vstr,&vlen,&vll, NULL);
1582	    if (vstr) return sdsnewlen(vstr,vlen);
1583	    return sdsfromlonglong(vll);
1584	}
1585	
1586	/* Return the key at the current iterator position as a new entry. */
1587	Entry *hashTypeCurrentObjectNewEntry(hashTypeIterator *hi, size_t *usable) {
1588	    char fieldBuf[LONG_STR_SIZE], valueBuf[LONG_STR_SIZE];
1589	    unsigned char *fieldStr, *valueStr;
1590	    unsigned int fieldLen, valueLen;
1591	    long long fieldLl, valueLl;
1592	    Entry *entry;
1593	
1594	    /* Get field */
1595	    hashTypeCurrentObject(hi, OBJ_HASH_KEY, &fieldStr, &fieldLen, &fieldLl, NULL);
1596	    if (!fieldStr) {
1597	        fieldLen = ll2string(fieldBuf, sizeof(fieldBuf), fieldLl);
1598	        fieldStr = (unsigned char *) fieldBuf;
1599	    }
1600	    sds field = sdsnewlen(fieldStr, fieldLen);
1601	
1602	    /* Get value */
1603	    hashTypeCurrentObject(hi, OBJ_HASH_VALUE, &valueStr, &valueLen, &valueLl, NULL);
1604	    if (!valueStr) {
1605	        valueLen = ll2string(valueBuf, sizeof(valueBuf), valueLl);
1606	        valueStr = (unsigned char *) valueBuf;
1607	    }
1608	    sds value = sdsnewlen(valueStr, valueLen);
1609	    int hasExpiry = (hi->expire_time != EB_EXPIRE_TIME_INVALID);
1610	
1611	    /* Create entry with field and value, using iterator's expire_time */
1612	    uint32_t entryFlags = ENTRY_TAKE_VALUE | ((hasExpiry) ? ENTRY_HAS_EXPIRY : 0); 
1613	    entry = entryCreate(field, value, entryFlags, usable);
1614	    sdsfree(field);  /* entryCreate() doesn't take ownership of field */
1615	
1616	    return entry;
1617	}
1618	
1619	static kvobj *hashTypeLookupWriteOrCreate(client *c, robj *key) {
1620	    dictEntryLink link;
1621	    kvobj *kv = lookupKeyWriteWithLink(c->db, key, &link);
1622	    if (checkType(c, kv, OBJ_HASH)) return NULL;
1623	
1624	    if (kv == NULL) {
1625	        robj *o = createHashObject();
1626	        kv = dbAddByLink(c->db, key, &o, &link);
1627	    }
1628	    return kv;
1629	}
1630	
1631	
1632	void hashTypeConvertListpack(robj *o, int enc) {
1633	    serverAssert(o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK);
1634	
1635	    if (enc == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1636	        /* Nothing to do... */
1637	
1638	    } else if (enc == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX) {
1639	        unsigned char *p;
1640	
1641	        /* Append HASH_LP_NO_TTL to each field name - value pair. */
1642	        p = lpFirst(o->ptr);
1643	        while (p != NULL) {
1644	            p = lpNext(o->ptr, p);
1645	            serverAssert(p);
1646	
1647	            o->ptr = lpInsertInteger(o->ptr, HASH_LP_NO_TTL, p, LP_AFTER, &p);
1648	            p = lpNext(o->ptr, p);
1649	        }
1650	
1651	        listpackEx *lpt = listpackExCreate();
1652	        lpt->lp = o->ptr;
1653	        o->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX;
1654	        o->ptr = lpt;
1655	    } else if (enc == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
1656	        hashTypeIterator hi;
1657	        dict *dict;
1658	        int ret;
1659	
1660	        hashTypeInitIterator(&hi, o);
1661	        dict = dictCreate(&entryHashDictType);
1662	
1663	        /* Presize the dict to avoid rehashing */
1664	        dictExpand(dict,hashTypeLength(o, 0));
1665	
1666	        size_t usable, *alloc_size = htGetMetadataSize(dict);
1667	        while (hashTypeNext(&hi, 0) != C_ERR) {
1668	            Entry *entry = hashTypeCurrentObjectNewEntry(&hi, &usable);
1669	            ret = dictAdd(dict, entry, NULL);
1670	            if (ret != DICT_OK) {
1671	                entryFree(entry, NULL); /* Needed for gcc ASAN */
1672	                hashTypeResetIterator(&hi);  /* Needed for gcc ASAN */
1673	                serverLogHexDump(LL_WARNING,"listpack with dup elements dump",
1674	                    o->ptr,lpBytes(o->ptr));
1675	                serverPanic("Listpack corruption detected");
1676	            }
1677	            *alloc_size += usable;
1678	        }
1679	        hashTypeResetIterator(&hi);
1680	        zfree(o->ptr);
1681	        o->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_HT;
1682	        o->ptr = dict;
1683	    } else {
1684	        serverPanic("Unknown hash encoding");
1685	    }
1686	}
1687	
1688	/* db can be NULL to avoid registration in subexpires */
