15	#include "server.h"
16	#include "intset.h"  /* Compact integer set structure */
17	#include "hyperloglog.h"
18	
19	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	 * Set Commands
21	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
22	
23	/* When estimating cardinality with HLL and a LIMIT is set, this is the minimum
24	 * number of additional elements to process between two hllCount() checks. */
25	#define HLL_CHECK_INTERVAL_FLOOR 1024
26	
27	/* Compute the UNION or DIFF (per 'op') of the 'setnum' sets named in 'setkeys'.
28	 *   dstkey          - if non-NULL, store the result into this key (…STORE) and
29	 *                     reply with its cardinality; otherwise reply to the client.
30	 *   cardinality_only - reply only with the result cardinality (SUNIONCARD),
31	 *                      without materializing the elements to the client.
32	 *   approx           - with cardinality_only, return an approximate cardinality
33	 *                      computed with a HyperLogLog (UNION only).
34	 *   limit            - with cardinality_only, stop once the cardinality reaches
35	 *                      'limit' (0 means no limit). */
36	void sunionDiffGenericCommand(client *c, robj **setkeys, int setnum,
37	                              robj *dstkey, int op,
38	                              int cardinality_only, int approx, long limit);
39	
40	/* Factory method to return a set that *can* hold "value". When the object has
41	 * an integer-encodable value, an intset will be returned. Otherwise a listpack
42	 * or a regular hash table.
43	 *
44	 * The size hint indicates approximately how many items will be added which is
45	 * used to determine the initial representation. */
46	robj *setTypeCreate(sds value, size_t size_hint) {
47	    if (isSdsRepresentableAsLongLong(value,NULL) == C_OK && size_hint <= server.set_max_intset_entries)
48	        return createIntsetObject();
49	    if (size_hint <= server.set_max_listpack_entries)
50	        return createSetListpackObject();
51	
52	    /* We may oversize the set by using the hint if the hint is not accurate,
53	     * but we will assume this is acceptable to maximize performance. */
54	    robj *o = createSetObject();
55	    dictExpand(o->ptr, size_hint);
56	    return o;
57	}
58	
59	/* Check if the existing set should be converted to another encoding based off the
60	 * the size hint. */
61	void setTypeMaybeConvert(robj *set, size_t size_hint) {
62	    if ((set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK && size_hint > server.set_max_listpack_entries)
63	        || (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET && size_hint > server.set_max_intset_entries))
64	    {
65	        setTypeConvertAndExpand(set, OBJ_ENCODING_HT, size_hint, 1);
66	    }
67	}
68	
69	/* Return the maximum number of entries to store in an intset. */
70	static size_t intsetMaxEntries(void) {
71	    size_t max_entries = server.set_max_intset_entries;
72	    /* limit to 1G entries due to intset internals. */
73	    if (max_entries >= 1<<30) max_entries = 1<<30;
74	    return max_entries;
75	}
76	
77	/* Converts intset to HT if it contains too many entries. */
78	static void maybeConvertIntset(robj *subject) {
79	    serverAssert(subject->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET);
80	    if (intsetLen(subject->ptr) > intsetMaxEntries())
81	        setTypeConvert(subject,OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
82	}
83	
84	/* When you know all set elements are integers, call this to convert the set to
85	 * an intset. No conversion happens if the set contains too many entries for an
86	 * intset. */
87	static void maybeConvertToIntset(robj *set) {
88	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) return; /* already intset */
89	    if (setTypeSize(set) > intsetMaxEntries()) return; /* can't use intset */
90	    intset *is = intsetNew();
91	    char *str;
92	    size_t len = 0;
93	    int64_t llval = 0;
94	    setTypeIterator si;
95	    setTypeInitIterator(&si, set);
96	    while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &llval) != -1) {
97	        if (str) {
98	            /* If the element is returned as a string, we may be able to convert
99	             * it to integer. This happens for OBJ_ENCODING_HT. */
100	            serverAssert(string2ll(str, len, (long long *)&llval));
101	        }
102	        uint8_t success = 0;
103	        is = intsetAdd(is, llval, &success);
104	        serverAssert(success);
105	    }
106	    setTypeResetIterator(&si);
107	    freeSetObject(set); /* frees the internals but not robj itself */
108	    set->ptr = is;
109	    set->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET;
110	}
111	
112	/* Add the specified sds value into a set.
113	 *
114	 * If the value was already member of the set, nothing is done and 0 is
115	 * returned, otherwise the new element is added and 1 is returned. */
116	int setTypeAdd(robj *subject, sds value) {
117	    return setTypeAddAux(subject, value, sdslen(value), 0, 1);
118	}
119	
120	/* Add member. This function is optimized for the different encodings. The
121	 * value can be provided as an sds string (indicated by passing str_is_sds =
122	 * 1), as string and length (str_is_sds = 0) or as an integer in which case str
123	 * is set to NULL and llval is provided instead.
124	 *
125	 * Returns 1 if the value was added and 0 if it was already a member. */
126	int setTypeAddAux(robj *set, char *str, size_t len, int64_t llval, int str_is_sds) {
127	    char tmpbuf[LONG_STR_SIZE];
128	    if (!str) {
129	        if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
130	            uint8_t success = 0;
131	            set->ptr = intsetAdd(set->ptr, llval, &success);
132	            if (success) maybeConvertIntset(set);
133	            return success;
134	        }
135	        /* Convert int to string. */
136	        len = ll2string(tmpbuf, sizeof tmpbuf, llval);
137	        str = tmpbuf;
138	        str_is_sds = 0;
139	    }
140	
141	    serverAssert(str);
142	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
143	        /* Avoid duping the string if it is an sds string. */
144	        sds sdsval = str_is_sds ? (sds)str : sdsnewlen(str, len);
145	        dict *ht = set->ptr;
146	        dictEntryLink bucket, link = dictFindLink(ht, sdsval, &bucket);
147	        if (link == NULL) {
148	            /* Key doesn't already exist in the set. Add it but dup the key. */
149	            if (sdsval == str) sdsval = sdsdup(sdsval);
150	            dictSetKeyAtLink(ht, sdsval, &bucket, 1);
151	            *htGetMetadataSize(ht) += sdsAllocSize(sdsval);
152	            return 1;
153	        } else if (sdsval != str) {
154	            /* String is already a member. Free our temporary sds copy. */
155	            sdsfree(sdsval);
156	            return 0;
157	        }
158	    } else if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
159	        unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
160	        unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
161	        if (p != NULL)
162	            p = lpFind(lp, p, (unsigned char*)str, len, 0);
163	        if (p == NULL) {
164	            /* Not found.  */
165	            if (lpLength(lp) < server.set_max_listpack_entries &&
166	                len <= server.set_max_listpack_value &&
167	                lpSafeToAdd(lp, len))
168	            {
169	                if (str == tmpbuf) {
170	                    /* This came in as integer so we can avoid parsing it again.
171	                     * TODO: Create and use lpFindInteger; don't go via string. */
172	                    lp = lpAppendInteger(lp, llval);
173	                } else {
174	                    lp = lpAppend(lp, (unsigned char*)str, len);
175	                }
176	                set->ptr = lp;
177	            } else {
178	                /* Size limit is reached. Convert to hashtable and add. */
179	                setTypeConvertAndExpand(set, OBJ_ENCODING_HT, lpLength(lp) + 1, 1);
180	                sds newval = sdsnewlen(str,len);
181	                serverAssert(dictAdd(set->ptr,newval,NULL) == DICT_OK);
182	                *htGetMetadataSize(set->ptr) += sdsAllocSize(newval);
183	            }
184	            return 1;
185	        }
186	    } else if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
187	        long long value;
188	        if (string2ll(str, len, &value)) {
189	            uint8_t success = 0;
190	            set->ptr = intsetAdd(set->ptr,value,&success);
191	            if (success) {
192	                maybeConvertIntset(set);
193	                return 1;
194	            }
195	        } else {
196	            /* Check if listpack encoding is safe not to cross any threshold. */
197	            size_t maxelelen = 0, totsize = 0;
198	            unsigned long n = intsetLen(set->ptr);
199	            if (n != 0) {
200	                size_t elelen1 = sdigits10(intsetMax(set->ptr));
201	                size_t elelen2 = sdigits10(intsetMin(set->ptr));
202	                maxelelen = max(elelen1, elelen2);
203	                size_t s1 = lpEstimateBytesRepeatedInteger(intsetMax(set->ptr), n);
204	                size_t s2 = lpEstimateBytesRepeatedInteger(intsetMin(set->ptr), n);
205	                totsize = max(s1, s2);
206	            }
207	            if (intsetLen((const intset*)set->ptr) < server.set_max_listpack_entries &&
208	                len <= server.set_max_listpack_value &&
209	                maxelelen <= server.set_max_listpack_value &&
210	                lpSafeToAdd(NULL, totsize + len))
211	            {
212	                /* In the "safe to add" check above we assumed all elements in
213	                 * the intset are of size maxelelen. This is an upper bound. */
214	                setTypeConvertAndExpand(set, OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK,
215	                                        intsetLen(set->ptr) + 1, 1);
216	                unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
217	                lp = lpAppend(lp, (unsigned char *)str, len);
218	                lp = lpShrinkToFit(lp);
219	                set->ptr = lp;
220	                return 1;
221	            } else {
222	                setTypeConvertAndExpand(set, OBJ_ENCODING_HT,
223	                                        intsetLen(set->ptr) + 1, 1);
224	                /* The set *was* an intset and this value is not integer
225	                 * encodable, so dictAdd should always work. */
226	                sds newval = sdsnewlen(str,len);
227	                serverAssert(dictAdd(set->ptr,newval,NULL) == DICT_OK);
228	                *htGetMetadataSize(set->ptr) += sdsAllocSize(newval);
229	                return 1;
230	            }
231	        }
232	    } else {
233	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
234	    }
235	    return 0;
236	}
237	
238	/* Deletes a value provided as an sds string from the set. Returns 1 if the
239	 * value was deleted and 0 if it was not a member of the set. */
240	int setTypeRemove(robj *setobj, sds value) {
241	    return setTypeRemoveAux(setobj, value, sdslen(value), 0, 1);
242	}
243	
244	/* Remove a member. This function is optimized for the different encodings. The
245	 * value can be provided as an sds string (indicated by passing str_is_sds =
246	 * 1), as string and length (str_is_sds = 0) or as an integer in which case str
247	 * is set to NULL and llval is provided instead.
248	 *
249	 * Returns 1 if the value was deleted and 0 if it was not a member of the set. */
250	int setTypeRemoveAux(robj *setobj, char *str, size_t len, int64_t llval, int str_is_sds) {
251	    char tmpbuf[LONG_STR_SIZE];
252	    if (!str) {
253	        if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
254	            int success;
255	            setobj->ptr = intsetRemove(setobj->ptr,llval,&success);
256	            return success;
257	        }
258	        len = ll2string(tmpbuf, sizeof tmpbuf, llval);
259	        str = tmpbuf;
260	        str_is_sds = 0;
261	    }
262	
263	    if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
264	        sds sdsval = str_is_sds ? (sds)str : sdsnewlen(str, len);
265	        int deleted = (dictDelete(setobj->ptr, sdsval) == DICT_OK);
266	        if (sdsval != str) sdsfree(sdsval); /* free temp copy */
267	        return deleted;
268	    } else if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
269	        unsigned char *lp = setobj->ptr;
270	        unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
271	        if (p == NULL) return 0;
272	        p = lpFind(lp, p, (unsigned char*)str, len, 0);
273	        if (p != NULL) {
274	            lp = lpDelete(lp, p, NULL);
275	            setobj->ptr = lp;
276	            return 1;
277	        }
278	    } else if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
279	        long long llval;
280	        if (string2ll(str, len, &llval)) {
281	            int success;
282	            setobj->ptr = intsetRemove(setobj->ptr,llval,&success);
283	            if (success) return 1;
284	        }
285	    } else {
286	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
287	    }
288	    return 0;
289	}
290	
291	/* Check if an sds string is a member of the set. Returns 1 if the value is a
292	 * member of the set and 0 if it isn't. */
293	int setTypeIsMember(robj *subject, sds value) {
294	    return setTypeIsMemberAux(subject, value, sdslen(value), 0, 1);
295	}
296	
297	/* Membership checking optimized for the different encodings. The value can be
298	 * provided as an sds string (indicated by passing str_is_sds = 1), as string
299	 * and length (str_is_sds = 0) or as an integer in which case str is set to NULL
300	 * and llval is provided instead.
301	 *
302	 * Returns 1 if the value is a member of the set and 0 if it isn't. */
303	int setTypeIsMemberAux(robj *set, char *str, size_t len, int64_t llval, int str_is_sds) {
304	    char tmpbuf[LONG_STR_SIZE];
305	    if (!str) {
306	        if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET)
307	            return intsetFind(set->ptr, llval);
308	        len = ll2string(tmpbuf, sizeof tmpbuf, llval);
309	        str = tmpbuf;
310	        str_is_sds = 0;
311	    }
312	
313	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
314	        unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
315	        unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
316	        return p && lpFind(lp, p, (unsigned char*)str, len, 0);
317	    } else if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
318	        long long llval;
319	        return string2ll(str, len, &llval) && intsetFind(set->ptr, llval);
320	    } else if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT && str_is_sds) {
321	        return dictFind(set->ptr, (sds)str) != NULL;
322	    } else if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
323	        sds sdsval = sdsnewlen(str, len);
324	        int result = dictFind(set->ptr, sdsval) != NULL;
325	        sdsfree(sdsval);
326	        return result;
327	    } else {
328	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
329	    }
330	}
331	
332	void setTypeInitIterator(setTypeIterator *si, robj *subject) {
333	    si->subject = subject;
334	    si->encoding = subject->encoding;
335	    if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
336	        dictInitIterator(&si->di, subject->ptr);
337	    } else if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
338	        si->ii = 0;
339	    } else if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
340	        si->lpi = NULL;
341	    } else {
342	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
343	    }
344	}
345	
346	void setTypeResetIterator(setTypeIterator *si) {
347	    if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT)
348	        dictResetIterator(&si->di);
349	}
350	
351	/* Move to the next entry in the set. Returns the object at the current
352	 * position, as a string or as an integer.
353	 *
354	 * Since set elements can be internally be stored as SDS strings, char buffers or
355	 * simple arrays of integers, setTypeNext returns the encoding of the
356	 * set object you are iterating, and will populate the appropriate pointers
357	 * (str and len) or (llele) depending on whether the value is stored as a string
358	 * or as an integer internally.
359	 *
360	 * If OBJ_ENCODING_HT is returned, then str points to an sds string and can be
361	 * used as such. If OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET, then llele is populated and str is
362	 * pointed to NULL. If OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK is returned, the value can be
363	 * either a string or an integer. If *str is not NULL, then str and len are
364	 * populated with the string content and length. Otherwise, llele populated with
365	 * an integer value.
366	 *
367	 * Note that str, len and llele pointers should all be passed and cannot
368	 * be NULL since the function will try to defensively populate the non
369	 * used field with values which are easy to trap if misused.
370	 *
371	 * When there are no more elements -1 is returned. */
372	int setTypeNext(setTypeIterator *si, char **str, size_t *len, int64_t *llele) {
373	    if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
374	        dictEntry *de = dictNext(&si->di);
375	        if (de == NULL) return -1;
376	        *str = dictGetKey(de);
377	        *len = sdslen(*str);
378	        *llele = -123456789; /* Not needed. Defensive. */
379	    } else if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
380	        if (!intsetGet(si->subject->ptr,si->ii++,llele))
381	            return -1;
382	        *str = NULL;
383	    } else if (si->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
384	        unsigned char *lp = si->subject->ptr;
385	        unsigned char *lpi = si->lpi;
386	        if (lpi == NULL) {
387	            lpi = lpFirst(lp);
388	        } else {
389	            lpi = lpNext(lp, lpi);
390	        }
391	        if (lpi == NULL) return -1;
392	        si->lpi = lpi;
393	        unsigned int l = 0;
394	        *str = (char *)lpGetValue(lpi, &l, (long long *)llele);
395	        *len = (size_t)l;
396	    } else {
397	        serverPanic("Wrong set encoding in setTypeNext");
398	    }
399	    return si->encoding;
400	}
401	
402	/* The not copy on write friendly version but easy to use version
403	 * of setTypeNext() is setTypeNextObject(), returning new SDS
404	 * strings. So if you don't retain a pointer to this object you should call
405	 * sdsfree() against it.
406	 *
407	 * This function is the way to go for write operations where COW is not
408	 * an issue. */
409	sds setTypeNextObject(setTypeIterator *si) {
410	    int64_t intele = 0;
411	    char *str;
412	    size_t len = 0;
413	
414	    if (setTypeNext(si, &str, &len, &intele) == -1) return NULL;
415	    if (str != NULL) return sdsnewlen(str, len);
416	    return sdsfromlonglong(intele);
417	}
418	
419	/* Return random element from a non empty set.
420	 * The returned element can be an int64_t value if the set is encoded
421	 * as an "intset" blob of integers, or an string.
422	 *
423	 * The caller provides three pointers to be populated with the right
424	 * object. The return value of the function is the object->encoding
425	 * field of the object and can be used by the caller to check if the
426	 * int64_t pointer or the str and len pointers were populated, as for
427	 * setTypeNext. If OBJ_ENCODING_HT is returned, str is pointed to a
428	 * string which is actually an sds string and it can be used as such.
429	 *
430	 * Note that both the str, len and llele pointers should be passed and cannot
431	 * be NULL. If str is set to NULL, the value is an integer stored in llele. */
432	int setTypeRandomElement(robj *setobj, char **str, size_t *len, int64_t *llele) {
433	    if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
434	        dictEntry *de = dictGetFairRandomKey(setobj->ptr);
435	        *str = dictGetKey(de);
436	        *len = sdslen(*str);
437	        *llele = -123456789; /* Not needed. Defensive. */
438	    } else if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
439	        *llele = intsetRandom(setobj->ptr);
440	        *str = NULL; /* Not needed. Defensive. */
441	    } else if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
442	        unsigned char *lp = setobj->ptr;
443	        int r = rand() % lpLength(lp);
444	        unsigned char *p = lpSeek(lp, r);
445	        unsigned int l;
446	        *str = (char *)lpGetValue(p, &l, (long long *)llele);
447	        *len = (size_t)l;
448	    } else {
449	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
450	    }
451	    return setobj->encoding;
452	}
453	
454	/* Pops a random element and returns it as an object. */
455	robj *setTypePopRandom(robj *set) {
456	    robj *obj;
457	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
458	        /* Find random and delete it without re-seeking the listpack. */
459	        unsigned int i = 0;
460	        unsigned char *p = lpNextRandom(set->ptr, lpFirst(set->ptr), &i, 1, 1);
461	        unsigned int len = 0; /* initialize to silence warning */
462	        long long llele = 0; /* initialize to silence warning */
463	        char *str = (char *)lpGetValue(p, &len, &llele);
464	        if (str)
465	            obj = createStringObject(str, len);
466	        else
467	            obj = createStringObjectFromLongLong(llele);
468	        set->ptr = lpDelete(set->ptr, p, NULL);
469	    } else {
470	        char *str;
471	        size_t len = 0;
472	        int64_t llele = 0;
473	        int encoding = setTypeRandomElement(set, &str, &len, &llele);
474	        if (str)
475	            obj = createStringObject(str, len);
476	        else
477	            obj = createStringObjectFromLongLong(llele);
478	        setTypeRemoveAux(set, str, len, llele, encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
479	    }
480	    return obj;
481	}
482	
483	unsigned long setTypeSize(const robj *subject) {
484	    if (subject->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
485	        return dictSize((const dict*)subject->ptr);
486	    } else if (subject->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
487	        return intsetLen((const intset*)subject->ptr);
488	    } else if (subject->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
489	        return lpLength((unsigned char *)subject->ptr);
490	    } else {
491	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
492	    }
493	}
494	
495	size_t setTypeAllocSize(const robj *o) {
496	    serverAssertWithInfo(NULL,o,o->type == OBJ_SET);
497	    size_t size = 0;
498	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
499	        dict *d = o->ptr;
500	        size += sizeof(dict) + dictMemUsage(d) + *htGetMetadataSize(d);
501	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
502	        size = intsetAllocSize(o->ptr);
503	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
504	        size = lpBytes(o->ptr);
505	    } else {
506	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
507	    }
508	    return size;
509	}
510	
511	/* Convert the set to specified encoding. The resulting dict (when converting
512	 * to a hash table) is presized to hold the number of elements in the original
513	 * set. */
514	void setTypeConvert(robj *setobj, int enc) {
515	    setTypeConvertAndExpand(setobj, enc, setTypeSize(setobj), 1);
516	}
517	
518	/* Converts a set to the specified encoding, pre-sizing it for 'cap' elements.
519	 * The 'panic' argument controls whether to panic on OOM (panic=1) or return
520	 * C_ERR on OOM (panic=0). If panic=1 is given, this function always returns
521	 * C_OK. */
522	int setTypeConvertAndExpand(robj *setobj, int enc, unsigned long cap, int panic) {
523	    setTypeIterator si;
524	    serverAssertWithInfo(NULL,setobj,setobj->type == OBJ_SET &&
525	                             setobj->encoding != enc);
526	
527	    if (enc == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
528	        dict *d = dictCreate(&setDictType);
529	        sds element;
530	
531	        /* Presize the dict to avoid rehashing */
532	        if (panic) {
533	            dictExpand(d, cap);
534	        } else if (dictTryExpand(d, cap) != DICT_OK) {
535	            dictRelease(d);
536	            return C_ERR;
537	        }
538	
539	        /* To add the elements we extract integers and create redis objects */
540	        size_t *alloc_size = htGetMetadataSize(d);
541	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, setobj);
542	        while ((element = setTypeNextObject(&si)) != NULL) {
543	            serverAssert(dictAdd(d,element,NULL) == DICT_OK);
544	            *alloc_size += sdsAllocSize(element);
545	        }
546	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
547	
548	        freeSetObject(setobj); /* frees the internals but not setobj itself */
549	        setobj->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_HT;
550	        setobj->ptr = d;
551	    } else if (enc == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
552	        /* Preallocate the minimum two bytes per element (enc/value + backlen) */
553	        size_t estcap = cap * 2;
554	        if (setobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET && setTypeSize(setobj) > 0) {
555	            /* If we're converting from intset, we have a better estimate. */
556	            size_t s1 = lpEstimateBytesRepeatedInteger(intsetMin(setobj->ptr), cap);
557	            size_t s2 = lpEstimateBytesRepeatedInteger(intsetMax(setobj->ptr), cap);
558	            estcap = max(s1, s2);
559	        }
560	        unsigned char *lp = lpNew(estcap);
561	        char *str;
562	        size_t len = 0;
563	        int64_t llele = 0;
564	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, setobj);
565	        while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &llele) != -1) {
566	            if (str != NULL)
567	                lp = lpAppend(lp, (unsigned char *)str, len);
568	            else
569	                lp = lpAppendInteger(lp, llele);
570	        }
571	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
572	
573	        freeSetObject(setobj); /* frees the internals but not setobj itself */
574	        setobj->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK;
575	        setobj->ptr = lp;
576	    } else {
577	        serverPanic("Unsupported set conversion");
578	    }
579	    return C_OK;
580	}
581	
582	/* This is a helper function for the COPY command.
583	 * Duplicate a set object, with the guarantee that the returned object
584	 * has the same encoding as the original one.
585	 *
586	 * The resulting object always has refcount set to 1 */
587	robj *setTypeDup(robj *o) {
588	    robj *set;
589	
590	    serverAssert(o->type == OBJ_SET);
591	
592	    /* Create a new set object that have the same encoding as the original object's encoding */
593	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
594	        intset *is = o->ptr;
595	        size_t size = intsetBlobLen(is);
596	        intset *newis = zmalloc(size);
597	        memcpy(newis,is,size);
598	        set = createObject(OBJ_SET, newis);
599	        set->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET;
600	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
601	        unsigned char *lp = o->ptr;
602	        size_t sz = lpBytes(lp);
603	        unsigned char *new_lp = zmalloc(sz);
604	        memcpy(new_lp, lp, sz);
605	        set = createObject(OBJ_SET, new_lp);
606	        set->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK;
607	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
608	        set = createSetObject();
609	        dict *d = o->ptr;
610	        dictExpand(set->ptr, dictSize(d));
611	        setTypeIterator si;
612	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, o);
613	        char *str;
614	        size_t len = 0;
615	        int64_t intobj = 0;
616	        while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &intobj) != -1) {
617	            setTypeAdd(set, (sds)str);
618	        }
619	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
620	    } else {
621	        serverPanic("Unknown set encoding");
622	    }
623	    return set;
624	}

626	void saddCommand(client *c) {
627	    kvobj *set;
628	    int j, added = 0;
629	    dictEntryLink link;
630	    size_t oldsize = 0;
631	
632	    set = lookupKeyWriteWithLink(c->db,c->argv[1], &link);
633	    if (checkType(c,set,OBJ_SET)) return;
634	    
635	    if (set == NULL) {
636	        robj *o = setTypeCreate(c->argv[2]->ptr, c->argc - 2);
637	        set = dbAddByLink(c->db, c->argv[1], &o, &link);
638	    } else {
639	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
640	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
641	        setTypeMaybeConvert(set, c->argc - 2);
642	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
643	            updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
644	    }
645	
646	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
647	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
648	    for (j = 2; j < c->argc; j++) {
649	        if (setTypeAdd(set,c->argv[j]->ptr)) added++;
650	    }
651	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
652	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
653	    if (added) {
654	        unsigned long size = setTypeSize(set);
655	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, size - added, size);
656	        keyModified(c,c->db,c->argv[1],set,1);
657	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"sadd",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
658	    }
659	    server.dirty += added;
660	    addReplyLongLong(c,added);
661	}
662	
663	void sremCommand(client *c) {
664	    int j, deleted = 0, keyremoved = 0;
665	    size_t oldsize = 0;
666	
667	    kvobj *set = lookupKeyWriteOrReply(c, c->argv[1], shared.czero);
668	    if (set == NULL || checkType(c, set, OBJ_SET))
669	        return;
670	
671	    unsigned long oldSize = setTypeSize(set);
672	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
673	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
674	
675	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT)
676	        dictPauseAutoResize((dict*)set->ptr);
677	    for (j = 2; j < c->argc; j++) {
678	        if (setTypeRemove(set,c->argv[j]->ptr)) {
679	            deleted++;
680	            if (setTypeSize(set) == 0) {
681	                if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
682	                    updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
683	                dbDeleteSkipKeysizesUpdate(c->db, c->argv[1]);
684	                keyremoved = 1;
685	                break;
686	            }
687	        }
688	    }
689	    if (!keyremoved && set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT) {
690	        dictResumeAutoResize((dict*)set->ptr);
691	        dictShrinkIfNeeded((dict*)set->ptr);
692	    }
693	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled && !keyremoved)
694	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
695	    if (deleted) {
696	        int64_t newSize = oldSize - deleted;
697	
698	        keyModified(c, c->db, c->argv[1], keyremoved ? NULL : set, 1);
699	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"srem",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
700	        if (keyremoved) {
701	            notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",c->argv[1],
702	                                c->db->id);
703	            newSize = -1; /* removed */
704	        }
705	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, oldSize, newSize);
706	        server.dirty += deleted;
707	    }
708	    addReplyLongLong(c,deleted);
709	}
710	
711	void smoveCommand(client *c) {
712	    robj *srcset, *dstset, *ele;
713	    size_t oldSrcAllocSize = 0, oldDstAllocSize = 0;
714	    srcset = lookupKeyWrite(c->db,c->argv[1]);
715	    dstset = lookupKeyWrite(c->db,c->argv[2]);
716	    ele = c->argv[3];
717	
718	    /* If the source key does not exist return 0 */
719	    if (srcset == NULL) {
720	        addReply(c,shared.czero);
721	        return;
722	    }
723	
724	    /* If the source key has the wrong type, or the destination key
725	     * is set and has the wrong type, return with an error. */
726	    if (checkType(c,srcset,OBJ_SET) ||
727	        checkType(c,dstset,OBJ_SET)) return;
728	
729	    /* If srcset and dstset are equal, SMOVE is a no-op */
730	    if (srcset == dstset) {
731	        addReply(c,setTypeIsMember(srcset,ele->ptr) ?
732	            shared.cone : shared.czero);
733	        return;
734	    }
735	
736	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
737	        oldSrcAllocSize = kvobjAllocSize(srcset);
738	    int deleted = setTypeRemove(srcset,ele->ptr);
739	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
740	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), srcset, oldSrcAllocSize, kvobjAllocSize(srcset));
741	    /* If the element cannot be removed from the src set, return 0. */
742	    if (!deleted) {
743	        addReply(c,shared.czero);
744	        return;
745	    }
746	    notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"srem",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
747	
748	    /* Update keysizes histogram */
749	    int64_t srcNewLen = setTypeSize(srcset), srcOldLen = srcNewLen + 1;
750	
751	    /* Remove the src set from the database when empty */
752	    if (srcNewLen == 0) {
753	        dbDeleteSkipKeysizesUpdate(c->db,c->argv[1]);
754	        srcNewLen = -1; /* removed */
755	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
756	    }
757	    updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, srcOldLen, srcNewLen);
758	
759	    /* Create the destination set when it doesn't exist */
760	    if (!dstset) {
761	        dstset = setTypeCreate(ele->ptr, 1);
762	        dbAdd(c->db, c->argv[2], &dstset);
763	    }
764	
765	    keyModified(c, c->db, c->argv[1], (srcNewLen > 0) ? srcset : NULL, 1);
766	    server.dirty++;
767	
768	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
769	        oldDstAllocSize = kvobjAllocSize(dstset);
770	    /* An extra key has changed when ele was successfully added to dstset */
771	    if (setTypeAdd(dstset,ele->ptr)) {
772	        unsigned long dstLen = setTypeSize(dstset);
773	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, dstLen - 1, dstLen);
774	        server.dirty++;
775	        keyModified(c,c->db,c->argv[2],dstset,1);
776	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"sadd",c->argv[2],c->db->id);
777	    }
778	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
779	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[2]->ptr), dstset, oldDstAllocSize, kvobjAllocSize(dstset));
780	    addReply(c,shared.cone);
781	}

802	     * sets, where SMISMEMBER should respond with a series of zeros. */

829	
830	/* Handle the "SPOP key <count>" variant. The normal version of the
831	 * command is handled by the spopCommand() function itself. */
832	
833	/* How many times bigger should be the set compared to the remaining size
834	 * for us to use the "create new set" strategy? Read later in the
835	 * implementation for more info. */
836	#define SPOP_MOVE_STRATEGY_MUL 5
837	
838	void spopWithCountCommand(client *c) {
839	    long l;
840	    unsigned long count, size, toRemove;
841	    size_t oldsize = 0;
842	
843	    /* Get the count argument */
844	    if (getPositiveLongFromObjectOrReply(c,c->argv[2],&l,NULL) != C_OK) return;
845	    count = (unsigned long) l;
846	
847	    /* Make sure a key with the name inputted exists, and that it's type is
848	     * indeed a kv. Otherwise, return nil */
849	    robj *set = lookupKeyWriteOrReply(c, c->argv[1], shared.emptyset[c->resp]);
850	    if (set == NULL || checkType(c, set, OBJ_SET)) return;
851	
852	    /* If count is zero, serve an empty set ASAP to avoid special
853	     * cases later. */
854	    if (count == 0) {
855	        addReply(c,shared.emptyset[c->resp]);
856	        return;
857	    }
858	
859	    size = setTypeSize(set);
860	    toRemove = (count >= size) ? size : count;
861	
862	    /* Generate an SPOP keyspace notification */
863	    notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"spop",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
864	    server.dirty += toRemove;
865	
866	    /* CASE 1:
867	     * The number of requested elements is greater than or equal to
868	     * the number of elements inside the set: simply return the whole set. */
869	    if (count >= size) {
870	        /* We just return the entire set */
871	        sunionDiffGenericCommand(c,c->argv+1,1,NULL,SET_OP_UNION,0,0,0);
872	
873	        /* Delete the set as it is now empty */
874	        dbDelete(c->db,c->argv[1]);
875	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
876	
877	        /* todo: Move the spop notification to be executed after the command logic. */
878	
879	        /* Propagate this command as a DEL or UNLINK operation */
880	        robj *aux = server.lazyfree_lazy_server_del ? shared.unlink : shared.del;
881	        rewriteClientCommandVector(c, 2, aux, c->argv[1]);
882	        keyModified(c,c->db,c->argv[1],NULL,1);
883	        return;
884	    }
885	
886	    /* Case 2 and 3 require to replicate SPOP as a set of SREM commands.
887	     * Prepare our replication argument vector. Also send the array length
888	     * which is common to both the code paths. */
889	    unsigned long batchsize = count > 1024 ? 1024 : count;
890	    robj **propargv = zmalloc(sizeof(robj *) * (2 + batchsize));
891	    propargv[0] = shared.srem;
892	    propargv[1] = c->argv[1];
893	    unsigned long propindex = 2;
894	    addReplySetLen(c,count);
895	
896	    /* Common iteration vars. */
897	    char *str;
898	    size_t len = 0;
899	    int64_t llele = 0;
900	    unsigned long remaining = size-count; /* Elements left after SPOP. */
901	
902	    /* If we are here, the number of requested elements is less than the
903	     * number of elements inside the set. Also we are sure that count < size.
904	     * Use two different strategies.
905	     *
906	     * CASE 2: The number of elements to return is small compared to the
907	     * set size. We can just extract random elements and return them to
908	     * the set. */
909	    if (remaining*SPOP_MOVE_STRATEGY_MUL > count &&
910	        set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK)
911	    {
912	        /* Specialized case for listpack. Traverse it only once. */
913	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
914	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
915	        unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
916	        unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
917	        unsigned int index = 0;
918	        unsigned char **ps = zmalloc(sizeof(char *) * count);
919	        for (unsigned long i = 0; i < count; i++) {
920	            p = lpNextRandom(lp, p, &index, count - i, 1);
921	            unsigned int len = 0;
922	            str = (char *)lpGetValue(p, &len, (long long *)&llele);
923	
924	            if (str) {
925	                addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
926	                propargv[propindex++] = createStringObject(str, len);
927	            } else {
928	                addReplyBulkLongLong(c, llele);
929	                propargv[propindex++] = createStringObjectFromLongLong(llele);
930	            }
931	            /* Replicate/AOF this command as an SREM operation */
932	            if (propindex == 2 + batchsize) {
933	                alsoPropagate(c->db->id, propargv, propindex, PROPAGATE_AOF | PROPAGATE_REPL);
934	                for (unsigned long j = 2; j < propindex; j++) {
935	                    decrRefCount(propargv[j]);
936	                }
937	                propindex = 2;
938	            }
939	
940	            /* Store pointer for later deletion and move to next. */
941	            ps[i] = p;
942	            p = lpNext(lp, p);
943	            index++;
944	        }
945	        lp = lpBatchDelete(lp, ps, count);
946	        zfree(ps);
947	        set->ptr = lp;
948	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, size, size - count);
949	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
950	            updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
951	    } else if (remaining*SPOP_MOVE_STRATEGY_MUL > count) {
952	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
953	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
954	        for (unsigned long i = 0; i < count; i++) {
955	            propargv[propindex] = setTypePopRandom(set);
956	            addReplyBulk(c, propargv[propindex]);
957	            propindex++;
958	            /* Replicate/AOF this command as an SREM operation */
959	            if (propindex == 2 + batchsize) {
960	                alsoPropagate(c->db->id, propargv, propindex, PROPAGATE_AOF | PROPAGATE_REPL);
961	                for (unsigned long j = 2; j < propindex; j++) {
962	                    decrRefCount(propargv[j]);
963	                }
964	                propindex = 2;
965	            }
966	        }
967	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, size, size - count);
968	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
969	            updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
970	    } else {
971	    /* CASE 3: The number of elements to return is very big, approaching
972	     * the size of the set itself. After some time extracting random elements
973	     * from such a set becomes computationally expensive, so we use
974	     * a different strategy, we extract random elements that we don't
975	     * want to return (the elements that will remain part of the set),
976	     * creating a new set as we do this (that will be stored as the original
977	     * set). Then we return the elements left in the original set and
978	     * release it. */
979	        robj *newset = NULL;
980	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
981	            oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
982	
983	        /* Create a new set with just the remaining elements. */
984	        if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
985	            /* Specialized case for listpack. Traverse it only once. */
986	            newset = createSetListpackObject();
987	            unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
988	            unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
989	            unsigned int index = 0;
990	            unsigned char **ps = zmalloc(sizeof(char *) * remaining);
991	            for (unsigned long i = 0; i < remaining; i++) {
992	                p = lpNextRandom(lp, p, &index, remaining - i, 1);
993	                unsigned int len = 0;
994	                str = (char *)lpGetValue(p, &len, (long long *)&llele);
995	                setTypeAddAux(newset, str, len, llele, 0);
996	                ps[i] = p;
997	                p = lpNext(lp, p);
998	                index++;
999	            }
1000	            lp = lpBatchDelete(lp, ps, remaining);
1001	            zfree(ps);
1002	            set->ptr = lp;
1003	        } else {
1004	            while(remaining--) {
1005	                int encoding = setTypeRandomElement(set, &str, &len, &llele);
1006	                if (!newset) {
1007	                    newset = str ? createSetListpackObject() : createIntsetObject();
1008	                }
1009	                setTypeAddAux(newset, str, len, llele, encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
1010	                setTypeRemoveAux(set, str, len, llele, encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
1011	            }
1012	        }
1013	
1014	        /* Transfer the old set to the client. */
1015	        setTypeIterator si;
1016	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, set);
1017	        while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &llele) != -1) {
1018	            if (str == NULL) {
1019	                addReplyBulkLongLong(c,llele);
1020	                propargv[propindex++] = createStringObjectFromLongLong(llele);
1021	            } else {
1022	                addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1023	                propargv[propindex++] = createStringObject(str, len);
1024	            }
1025	            /* Replicate/AOF this command as an SREM operation */
1026	            if (propindex == 2 + batchsize) {
1027	                alsoPropagate(c->db->id, propargv, propindex, PROPAGATE_AOF | PROPAGATE_REPL);
1028	                for (unsigned long i = 2; i < propindex; i++) {
1029	                    decrRefCount(propargv[i]);
1030	                }
1031	                propindex = 2;
1032	            }
1033	        }
1034	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
1035	
1036	        /* Update key size histogram "explicitly" and not indirectly by dbReplaceValue()
1037	         * since function dbReplaceValue() assumes the entire set is being replaced, 
1038	         * but here we're building the new set from the existing one. As a result, 
1039	         * the size of the old set has already changed by the time we reach this point. */
1040	        updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, size, size-count);
1041	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1042	            updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
1043	        dbReplaceValue(c->db, c->argv[1], &newset, 0);
1044	        set = newset;
1045	    }
1046	
1047	    /* Replicate/AOF the remaining elements as an SREM operation */
1048	    if (propindex != 2) {
1049	        alsoPropagate(c->db->id, propargv, propindex, PROPAGATE_AOF | PROPAGATE_REPL);
1050	        for (unsigned long i = 2; i < propindex; i++) {
1051	            decrRefCount(propargv[i]);
1052	        }
1053	        propindex = 2;
1054	    }
1055	    zfree(propargv);
1056	
1057	    /* Don't propagate the command itself even if we incremented the
1058	     * dirty counter. We don't want to propagate an SPOP command since
1059	     * we propagated the command as a set of SREMs operations using
1060	     * the alsoPropagate() API. */
1061	    preventCommandPropagation(c);
1062	    keyModified(c,c->db,c->argv[1],set,1);
1063	}
1064	
1065	void spopCommand(client *c) {
1066	    unsigned long size;
1067	    robj *ele;
1068	    size_t oldsize = 0;
1069	
1070	    if (c->argc == 3) {
1071	        spopWithCountCommand(c);
1072	        return;
1073	    } else if (c->argc > 3) {
1074	        addReplyErrorObject(c,shared.syntaxerr);
1075	        return;
1076	    }
1077	
1078	    /* Make sure a key with the name inputted exists, and that it's type is
1079	     * indeed a kv */
1080	    kvobj *kv = lookupKeyWriteOrReply(c, c->argv[1], shared.null[c->resp]);
1081	    if (kv == NULL || checkType(c, kv, OBJ_SET)) return;
1082	
1083	    size = setTypeSize(kv);
1084	    updateKeysizesHist(c->db, OBJ_SET, size, size-1);
1085	
1086	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1087	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(kv);
1088	
1089	    /* Pop a random element from the kv */
1090	    ele = setTypePopRandom(kv);
1091	
1092	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1093	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), kv, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(kv));
1094	
1095	    notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"spop",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
1096	
1097	    /* Replicate/AOF this command as an SREM operation */
1098	    rewriteClientCommandVector(c,3,shared.srem,c->argv[1],ele);
1099	
1100	    /* Add the element to the reply */
1101	    addReplyBulk(c, ele);
1102	    decrRefCount(ele);
1103	
1104	    /* Delete the kv if it's empty */
1105	    int deleted = 0;
1106	    if (setTypeSize(kv) == 0) {
1107	        deleted = 1;
1108	        dbDelete(c->db,c->argv[1]);
1109	        notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",c->argv[1],c->db->id);
1110	    }
1111	
1112	    /* Set has been modified */
1113	    keyModified(c, c->db, c->argv[1], deleted ? NULL : kv, 1);
1114	    server.dirty++;
1115	}
1116	
1117	/* handle the "SRANDMEMBER key <count>" variant. The normal version of the
1118	 * command is handled by the srandmemberCommand() function itself. */
1119	
1120	/* How many times bigger should be the set compared to the requested size
1121	 * for us to don't use the "remove elements" strategy? Read later in the
1122	 * implementation for more info. */
1123	#define SRANDMEMBER_SUB_STRATEGY_MUL 3
1124	
1125	/* If client is trying to ask for a very large number of random elements,
1126	 * queuing may consume an unlimited amount of memory, so we want to limit
1127	 * the number of randoms per time. */
1128	#define SRANDFIELD_RANDOM_SAMPLE_LIMIT 1000
1129	
1130	void srandmemberWithCountCommand(client *c) {
1131	    long l;
1132	    unsigned long count, size;
1133	    int uniq = 1;
1134	    kvobj *set;
1135	    char *str;
1136	    size_t len = 0;
1137	    int64_t llele = 0;
1138	
1139	    dict *d;
1140	
1141	    if (getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c,c->argv[2],-LONG_MAX,LONG_MAX,&l,NULL) != C_OK) return;
1142	    if (l >= 0) {
1143	        count = (unsigned long) l;
1144	    } else {
1145	        /* A negative count means: return the same elements multiple times
1146	         * (i.e. don't remove the extracted element after every extraction). */
1147	        count = -l;
1148	        uniq = 0;
1149	    }
1150	
1151	    if ((set = lookupKeyReadOrReply(c,c->argv[1],shared.emptyarray))
1152	        == NULL || checkType(c,set,OBJ_SET)) return;
1153	    size = setTypeSize(set);
1154	
1155	    /* If count is zero, serve it ASAP to avoid special cases later. */
1156	    if (count == 0) {
1157	        addReply(c,shared.emptyarray);
1158	        return;
1159	    }
1160	
1161	    /* CASE 1: The count was negative, so the extraction method is just:
1162	     * "return N random elements" sampling the whole set every time.
1163	     * This case is trivial and can be served without auxiliary data
1164	     * structures. This case is the only one that also needs to return the
1165	     * elements in random order. */
1166	    if (!uniq || count == 1) {
1167	        addReplyArrayLen(c,count);
1168	
1169	        if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK && count > 1) {
1170	            /* Specialized case for listpack, traversing it only once. */
1171	            unsigned long limit, sample_count;
1172	            limit = count > SRANDFIELD_RANDOM_SAMPLE_LIMIT ? SRANDFIELD_RANDOM_SAMPLE_LIMIT : count;
1173	            listpackEntry *entries = zmalloc(limit * sizeof(listpackEntry));
1174	            while (count) {
1175	                sample_count = count > limit ? limit : count;
1176	                count -= sample_count;
1177	                lpRandomEntries(set->ptr, sample_count, entries);
1178	                for (unsigned long i = 0; i < sample_count; i++) {
1179	                    if (entries[i].sval)
1180	                        addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, entries[i].sval, entries[i].slen);
1181	                    else
1182	                        addReplyBulkLongLong(c, entries[i].lval);
1183	                }
1184	                if (c->flags & CLIENT_CLOSE_ASAP)
1185	                    break;
1186	            }
1187	            zfree(entries);
1188	            return;
1189	        }
1190	
1191	        while(count--) {
1192	            setTypeRandomElement(set, &str, &len, &llele);
1193	            if (str == NULL) {
1194	                addReplyBulkLongLong(c,llele);
1195	            } else {
1196	                addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1197	            }
1198	            if (c->flags & CLIENT_CLOSE_ASAP)
1199	                break;
1200	        }
1201	        return;
1202	    }
1203	
1204	    /* CASE 2:
1205	     * The number of requested elements is greater than the number of
1206	     * elements inside the set: simply return the whole set. */
1207	    if (count >= size) {
1208	        setTypeIterator si;
1209	        addReplyArrayLen(c,size);
1210	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, set);
1211	        while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &llele) != -1) {
1212	            if (str == NULL) {
1213	                addReplyBulkLongLong(c,llele);
1214	            } else {
1215	                addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1216	            }
1217	            size--;
1218	        }
1219	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
1220	        serverAssert(size==0);
1221	        return;
1222	    }
1223	
1224	    /* CASE 2.5 listpack only. Sampling unique elements, in non-random order.
1225	     * Listpack encoded sets are meant to be relatively small, so
1226	     * SRANDMEMBER_SUB_STRATEGY_MUL isn't necessary and we rather not make
1227	     * copies of the entries. Instead, we emit them directly to the output
1228	     * buffer.
1229	     *
1230	     * And it is inefficient to repeatedly pick one random element from a
1231	     * listpack in CASE 4. So we use this instead. */
1232	    if (set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1233	        unsigned char *lp = set->ptr;
1234	        unsigned char *p = lpFirst(lp);
1235	        unsigned int i = 0;
1236	        addReplyArrayLen(c, count);
1237	        while (count) {
1238	            p = lpNextRandom(lp, p, &i, count--, 1);
1239	            unsigned int len;
1240	            str = (char *)lpGetValue(p, &len, (long long *)&llele);
1241	            if (str == NULL) {
1242	                addReplyBulkLongLong(c, llele);
1243	            } else {
1244	                addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1245	            }
1246	            p = lpNext(lp, p);
1247	            i++;
1248	        }
1249	        return;
1250	    }
1251	
1252	    /* For CASE 3 and CASE 4 we need an auxiliary dictionary. */
1253	    d = dictCreate(&sdsReplyDictType);
1254	
1255	    /* CASE 3:
1256	     * The number of elements inside the set is not greater than
1257	     * SRANDMEMBER_SUB_STRATEGY_MUL times the number of requested elements.
1258	     * In this case we create a set from scratch with all the elements, and
1259	     * subtract random elements to reach the requested number of elements.
1260	     *
1261	     * This is done because if the number of requested elements is just
1262	     * a bit less than the number of elements in the set, the natural approach
1263	     * used into CASE 4 is highly inefficient. */
1264	    if (count*SRANDMEMBER_SUB_STRATEGY_MUL > size) {
1265	        setTypeIterator si;
1266	
1267	        /* Add all the elements into the temporary dictionary. */
1268	        setTypeInitIterator(&si, set);
1269	        dictExpand(d, size);
1270	        while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &llele) != -1) {
1271	            int retval = DICT_ERR;
1272	
1273	            if (str == NULL) {
1274	                retval = dictAdd(d,sdsfromlonglong(llele),NULL);
1275	            } else {
1276	                retval = dictAdd(d, sdsnewlen(str, len), NULL);
1277	            }
1278	            serverAssert(retval == DICT_OK);
1279	        }
1280	        setTypeResetIterator(&si);
1281	        serverAssert(dictSize(d) == size);
1282	
1283	        /* Remove random elements to reach the right count. */
1284	        while (size > count) {
1285	            dictEntry *de;
1286	            de = dictGetFairRandomKey(d);
1287	            dictUnlink(d,dictGetKey(de));
1288	            sdsfree(dictGetKey(de));
1289	            dictFreeUnlinkedEntry(d,de);
1290	            size--;
1291	        }
1292	    }
1293	
1294	    /* CASE 4: We have a big set compared to the requested number of elements.
1295	     * In this case we can simply get random elements from the set and add
1296	     * to the temporary set, trying to eventually get enough unique elements
1297	     * to reach the specified count. */
1298	    else {
1299	        unsigned long added = 0;
1300	        sds sdsele;
1301	
1302	        dictExpand(d, count);
1303	        while (added < count) {
1304	            setTypeRandomElement(set, &str, &len, &llele);
1305	            if (str == NULL) {
1306	                sdsele = sdsfromlonglong(llele);
1307	            } else {
1308	                sdsele = sdsnewlen(str, len);
1309	            }
1310	            /* Try to add the object to the dictionary. If it already exists
1311	             * free it, otherwise increment the number of objects we have
1312	             * in the result dictionary. */
1313	            if (dictAdd(d,sdsele,NULL) == DICT_OK)
1314	                added++;
1315	            else
1316	                sdsfree(sdsele);
1317	        }
1318	    }
1319	
1320	    /* CASE 3 & 4: send the result to the user. */
1321	    {
1322	        dictIterator di;
1323	        dictEntry *de;
1324	
1325	        addReplyArrayLen(c,count);
1326	        dictInitIterator(&di, d);
1327	        while((de = dictNext(&di)) != NULL)
1328	            addReplyBulkSds(c,dictGetKey(de));
1329	        dictResetIterator(&di);
1330	        dictRelease(d);
1331	    }
1332	}
1333	
1334	/* SRANDMEMBER <key> [<count>] */
1335	void srandmemberCommand(client *c) {
1336	    kvobj *set;
1337	    char *str;
1338	    size_t len = 0;
1339	    int64_t llele = 0;
1340	    size_t oldsize = 0;
1341	
1342	    if (c->argc == 3) {
1343	        srandmemberWithCountCommand(c);
1344	        return;
1345	    } else if (c->argc > 3) {
1346	        addReplyErrorObject(c,shared.syntaxerr);
1347	        return;
1348	    }
1349	
1350	    /* Handle variant without <count> argument. Reply with simple bulk string */
1351	    if ((set = lookupKeyReadOrReply(c,c->argv[1],shared.null[c->resp]))
1352	        == NULL || checkType(c,set,OBJ_SET)) return;
1353	
1354	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1355	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(set);
1356	    setTypeRandomElement(set, &str, &len, &llele);
1357	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1358	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), set, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(set));
1359	    if (str == NULL) {
1360	        addReplyBulkLongLong(c,llele);
1361	    } else {
1362	        addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1363	    }
1364	}
1365	
1366	typedef struct setopsrc {
1367	    robj *set;
1368	    size_t oldsize;
1369	} setopsrc;
1370	
1371	int qsortCompareSetsByCardinality(const void *s1, const void *s2) {
1372	    robj *o1 = ((setopsrc*)s1)->set, *o2 = ((setopsrc*)s2)->set;
1373	    if (setTypeSize(o1) > setTypeSize(o2)) return 1;
1374	    if (setTypeSize(o1) < setTypeSize(o2)) return -1;
1375	    return 0;
1376	}
1377	
1378	/* This is used by SDIFF and in this case we can receive NULL that should
1379	 * be handled as empty sets. */
1380	int qsortCompareSetsByRevCardinality(const void *s1, const void *s2) {
1381	    robj *o1 = ((setopsrc*)s1)->set, *o2 = ((setopsrc*)s2)->set;
1382	    unsigned long first = o1 ? setTypeSize(o1) : 0;
1383	    unsigned long second = o2 ? setTypeSize(o2) : 0;
1384	
1385	    if (first < second) return 1;
1386	    if (first > second) return -1;
1387	    return 0;
1388	}
1389	
1390	/* SINTER / SINTERSTORE / SINTERCARD
1391	 *
1392	 * 'cardinality_only' work for SINTERCARD, only return the cardinality
1393	 * with minimum processing and memory overheads.
1394	 *
1395	 * 'limit' work for SINTERCARD, stop searching after reaching the limit.
1396	 * Passing a 0 means unlimited.
1397	 */
1398	void sinterGenericCommand(client *c, robj **setkeys,
1399	                          unsigned long setnum, robj *dstkey,
1400	                          int cardinality_only, unsigned long limit) {
1401	    setopsrc *sets = zmalloc(sizeof(setopsrc)*setnum);
1402	    setTypeIterator si;
1403	    robj *dstset = NULL;
1404	    char *str;
1405	    size_t len = 0;
1406	    int64_t intobj = 0;
1407	    void *replylen = NULL;
1408	    unsigned long j, cardinality = 0;
1409	    int encoding, empty = 0;
1410	
1411	    for (j = 0; j < setnum; j++) {
1412	        kvobj *kv = lookupKeyRead(c->db, setkeys[j]);
1413	        if (!kv) {
1414	            /* A NULL is considered an empty set */
1415	            empty += 1;
1416	            sets[j].set = NULL;
1417	            sets[j].oldsize = 0;
1418	            continue;
1419	        }
1420	        if (checkType(c, kv, OBJ_SET)) {
1421	            zfree(sets);
1422	            return;
1423	        }
1424	        sets[j].set = kv;
1425	        if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1426	            sets[j].oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(kv);
1427	    }
1428	
1429	    /* Set intersection with an empty set always results in an empty set.
1430	     * Return ASAP if there is an empty set. */
1431	    if (empty > 0) {
1432	        zfree(sets);
1433	        if (dstkey) {
1434	            if (dbDelete(c->db,dstkey)) {
1435	                keyModified(c,c->db,dstkey,NULL,1);
1436	                notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",dstkey,c->db->id);
1437	                server.dirty++;
1438	            }
1439	            addReply(c,shared.czero);
1440	        } else if (cardinality_only) {
1441	            addReplyLongLong(c,cardinality);
1442	        } else {
1443	            addReply(c,shared.emptyset[c->resp]);
1444	        }
1445	        return;
1446	    }
1447	
1448	    /* Sort sets from the smallest to largest, this will improve our
1449	     * algorithm's performance */
1450	    qsort(sets,setnum,sizeof(setopsrc),qsortCompareSetsByCardinality);
1451	
1452	    /* The first thing we should output is the total number of elements...
1453	     * since this is a multi-bulk write, but at this stage we don't know
1454	     * the intersection set size, so we use a trick, append an empty object
1455	     * to the output list and save the pointer to later modify it with the
1456	     * right length */
1457	    if (dstkey) {
1458	        /* If we have a target key where to store the resulting set
1459	         * create this key with an empty set inside */
1460	        if (sets[0].set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET) {
1461	            /* The first set is an intset, so the result is an intset too. The
1462	             * elements are inserted in ascending order which is efficient in an
1463	             * intset. */
1464	            dstset = createIntsetObject();
1465	        } else if (sets[0].set->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1466	            /* To avoid many reallocs, we estimate that the result is a listpack
1467	             * of approximately the same size as the first set. Then we shrink
1468	             * it or possibly convert it to intset in the end. */
1469	            unsigned char *lp = lpNew(lpBytes(sets[0].set->ptr));
1470	            dstset = createObject(OBJ_SET, lp);
1471	            dstset->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK;
1472	        } else {
1473	            /* We start off with a listpack, since it's more efficient to append
1474	             * to than an intset. Later we can convert it to intset or a
1475	             * hashtable. */
1476	            dstset = createSetListpackObject();
1477	        }
1478	    } else if (!cardinality_only) {
1479	        replylen = addReplyDeferredLen(c);
1480	    }
1481	
1482	    /* Iterate all the elements of the first (smallest) set, and test
1483	     * the element against all the other sets, if at least one set does
1484	     * not include the element it is discarded */
1485	    int only_integers = 1;
1486	    setTypeInitIterator(&si, sets[0].set);
1487	    while((encoding = setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &intobj)) != -1) {
1488	        for (j = 1; j < setnum; j++) {
1489	            if (sets[j].set == sets[0].set) continue;
1490	            if (!setTypeIsMemberAux(sets[j].set, str, len, intobj,
1491	                                    encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT))
1492	                break;
1493	        }
1494	
1495	        /* Only take action when all sets contain the member */
1496	        if (j == setnum) {
1497	            if (cardinality_only) {
1498	                cardinality++;
1499	
1500	                /* We stop the searching after reaching the limit. */
1501	                if (limit && cardinality >= limit)
1502	                    break;
1503	            } else if (!dstkey) {
1504	                if (str != NULL)
1505	                    addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1506	                else
1507	                    addReplyBulkLongLong(c,intobj);
1508	                cardinality++;
1509	            } else {
1510	                if (str && only_integers) {
1511	                    /* It may be an integer although we got it as a string. */
1512	                    if (encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT &&
1513	                        string2ll(str, len, (long long *)&intobj))
1514	                    {
1515	                        if (dstset->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK ||
1516	                            dstset->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INTSET)
1517	                        {
1518	                            /* Adding it as an integer is more efficient. */
1519	                            str = NULL;
1520	                        }
1521	                    } else {
1522	                        /* It's not an integer */
1523	                        only_integers = 0;
1524	                    }
1525	                }
1526	                setTypeAddAux(dstset, str, len, intobj, encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_HT);
1527	            }
1528	        }
1529	    }
1530	    setTypeResetIterator(&si);
1531	
1532	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled) {
1533	        for (j = 0; j < setnum; j++) {
1534	            robj *obj = sets[j].set;
1535	            if (!obj) continue;
1536	            updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(setkeys[j]->ptr), obj,
1537	                                sets[j].oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(obj));
1538	        }
1539	    }
1540	
1541	    if (cardinality_only) {
1542	        addReplyLongLong(c,cardinality);
1543	    } else if (dstkey) {
1544	        /* Store the resulting set into the target, if the intersection
1545	         * is not an empty set. */
1546	        if (setTypeSize(dstset) > 0) {
1547	            if (only_integers) maybeConvertToIntset(dstset);
1548	            if (dstset->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1549	                /* We allocated too much memory when we created it to avoid
1550	                 * frequent reallocs. Therefore, we shrink it now. */
1551	                dstset->ptr = lpShrinkToFit(dstset->ptr);
1552	            }
1553	            setKey(c, c->db, dstkey, &dstset, 0);
1554	            addReplyLongLong(c,setTypeSize(dstset));
1555	            notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_SET,"sinterstore",
1556	                dstkey,c->db->id);
1557	            server.dirty++;
1558	        } else {
1559	            addReply(c,shared.czero);
1560	            if (dbDelete(c->db,dstkey)) {
1561	                server.dirty++;
1562	                keyModified(c,c->db,dstkey,NULL,1);
1563	                notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC,"del",dstkey,c->db->id);
1564	            }
1565	            decrRefCount(dstset);
1566	        }
1567	    } else {
1568	        setDeferredSetLen(c,replylen,cardinality);
1569	    }
1570	    zfree(sets);
1571	}
1572	
1573	/* SINTER key [key ...] */
1574	void sinterCommand(client *c) {
1575	    sinterGenericCommand(c, c->argv+1,  c->argc-1, NULL, 0, 0);
1576	}
1577	
1578	/* SMEMBERS key */
1579	void smembersCommand(client *c) {
1580	    setTypeIterator si;
1581	    char *str;
1582	    size_t len = 0;
1583	    int64_t intobj = 0;
1584	    size_t oldsize = 0;
1585	    kvobj *setobj = lookupKeyRead(c->db, c->argv[1]);
1586	    if (checkType(c,setobj,OBJ_SET)) return;
1587	    if (!setobj) {
1588	        addReply(c, shared.emptyset[c->resp]);
1589	        return;
1590	    }
1591	
1592	    /* Prepare the response. */
1593	    unsigned long length = setTypeSize(setobj);
1594	    addReplySetLen(c,length);
1595	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1596	        oldsize = kvobjAllocSize(setobj);
1597	    /* Iterate through the elements of the set. */
1598	    setTypeInitIterator(&si, setobj);
1599	
1600	    while (setTypeNext(&si, &str, &len, &intobj) != -1) {
1601	        if (str != NULL)
1602	            addReplyBulkCBuffer(c, str, len);
1603	        else
1604	            addReplyBulkLongLong(c, intobj);
1605	        length--;
1606	    }
1607	    setTypeResetIterator(&si);
1608	    if (server.memory_tracking_enabled)
1609	        updateSlotAllocSize(c->db, getKeySlot(c->argv[1]->ptr), setobj, oldsize, kvobjAllocSize(setobj));
1610	    serverAssert(length == 0); /* fail on corrupt data */
1611	}
1612	
1613	/* SINTERCARD numkeys key [key ...] [LIMIT limit] */
1614	void sinterCardCommand(client *c) {
1615	    long j;
1616	    long numkeys = 0; /* Number of keys. */
1617	    long limit = 0;   /* 0 means not limit. */
1618	
1619	    if (getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c, c->argv[1], 1, LONG_MAX,
1620	                                      &numkeys, "numkeys should be greater than 0") != C_OK)
1621	        return;
1622	    if (numkeys > (c->argc - 2)) {
1623	        addReplyError(c, "Number of keys can't be greater than number of args");
1624	        return;
1625	    }
1626	
1627	    for (j = 2 + numkeys; j < c->argc; j++) {
1628	        char *opt = c->argv[j]->ptr;
1629	        int moreargs = (c->argc - 1) - j;
1630	
1631	        if (!strcasecmp(opt, "LIMIT") && moreargs) {
1632	            j++;
1633	            if (getPositiveLongFromObjectOrReply(c, c->argv[j], &limit,
1634	                                                 "LIMIT can't be negative") != C_OK)
1635	                return;
1636	        } else {
1637	            addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.syntaxerr);
1638	            return;
1639	        }
1640	    }
1641	
1642	    sinterGenericCommand(c, c->argv+2, numkeys, NULL, 1, limit);
1643	}
1644	
1645	/* SINTERSTORE destination key [key ...] */
1646	void sinterstoreCommand(client *c) {
1647	    sinterGenericCommand(c, c->argv+2, c->argc-2, c->argv[1], 0, 0);
1648	}
1649	
1650	void sunionDiffGenericCommand(client *c, robj **setkeys, int setnum,
1651	                              robj *dstkey, int op,
1652	                              int cardinality_only, int approx, long limit)
1653	{
1654	    /* Approximate cardinality is only ever requested for SUNIONCARD, i.e. a
1655	     * non-storing UNION that returns a count. */

1665	    int64_t llval = 0;
