15	
16	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	 * Sorted set API
18	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
19	
20	/* ZSETs are ordered sets using two data structures to hold the same elements
21	 * in order to get O(log(N)) INSERT and REMOVE operations into a sorted
22	 * data structure.
23	 *
24	 * The elements are added to a hash table mapping Redis objects to scores.
25	 * At the same time the elements are added to a skip list mapping scores
26	 * to Redis objects (so objects are sorted by scores in this "view").
27	 *
28	 * Note that the SDS string representing the element is the same in both
29	 * the hash table and skiplist in order to save memory. What we do in order
30	 * to manage the shared SDS string more easily is to free the SDS string
31	 * only in zslFreeNode(). The dictionary has no value free method set.
32	 * So we should always remove an element from the dictionary, and later from
33	 * the skiplist.
34	 *
35	 * This skiplist implementation is almost a C translation of the original
36	 * algorithm described by William Pugh in "Skip Lists: A Probabilistic
37	 * Alternative to Balanced Trees", modified in three ways:
38	 * a) this implementation allows for repeated scores.
39	 * b) the comparison is not just by key (our 'score') but by satellite data.
40	 * c) there is a back pointer, so it's a doubly linked list with the back
41	 * pointers being only at "level 1". This allows to traverse the list
42	 * from tail to head, useful for ZREVRANGE. */
43	#include "fast_float_strtod.h"
44	#include "server.h"
45	#include "intset.h"  /* Compact integer set structure */
46	#include <math.h>
47	
48	#define ZSL_OFFSET_MAX_ELE  UINT16_MAX
49	#define ZSL_OFFSET_NO_ELE   UINT16_MAX
50	
51	const void *zslGetNodeElementForDict(const void *node);
52	
53	/* dictType for zset's dict (maps sds to zskiplistNode*) */
54	dictType zsetDictType = {
55	    dictSdsHash,        /* hash function */
56	    NULL,               /* key dup */
57	    NULL,               /* val dup */
58	    dictSdsKeyCompare,  /* compares embedded sds by keyFromStoredKey */
59	    NULL,               /* key destructor - skiplist owns the node memory */
60	    NULL,               /* val destructor */
61	    NULL,               /* allow to expand */
62	    .no_value = 1,      /* no values stored (only nodes) */
63	    .keyFromStoredKey = zslGetNodeElementForDict,  /* extract embedded sds from node */
64	};
65	
66	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
67	 * Skiplist implementation of the low level API
68	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
69	
70	int zslLexValueGteMin(sds value, zlexrangespec *spec);
71	int zslLexValueLteMax(sds value, zlexrangespec *spec);
72	void zsetConvertAndExpand(robj *zobj, int encoding, unsigned long cap);
73	static zskiplistNode *zslGetElementByRankFromNode(zskiplistNode *start_node, int start_level, unsigned long rank);
74	
75	static inline unsigned long zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(zskiplistNode *x, int level) {
76	    /* At level 0, span stores node level instead of distance, so return the actual span value:
77	     * 1 for all nodes except the last node (which has span 0). */
78	    if (level > 0) return x->level[level].span;
79	    /* For level 0, if regular node, span is 1. If tail node, span is 0. */
80	    return x->level[0].forward ? 1 : 0;
81	}
82	
83	static inline void zslSetNodeSpanAtLevel(zskiplistNode *x, int level, unsigned long span) {
84	    /* Skip level 0 since it stores node level, not span. */
85	    if (level > 0)
86	        x->level[level].span = span;
87	}
88	
89	static inline void zslIncrNodeSpanAtLevel(zskiplistNode *x, int level, unsigned long incr) {
90	    /* Skip level 0 since it stores node level, not span. */
91	    if (level > 0)
92	        x->level[level].span += incr;
93	}
94	
95	static inline void zslDecrNodeSpanAtLevel(zskiplistNode *x, int level, unsigned long decr) {
96	    /* Skip level 0 since it stores node level, not span. */
97	    if (level > 0)
98	        x->level[level].span -= decr;
99	}
100	
101	/* Get zskiplistNodeInfo from node (stored in level[0].span). */
102	static_assert(sizeof(zskiplistNodeInfo) <= sizeof(((zskiplistNode *)0)->level[0].span), "Must fit in level[0].span");
103	static inline zskiplistNodeInfo *zslGetNodeInfo(const zskiplistNode *node) {
104	    return (zskiplistNodeInfo *)&node->level[0].span;
105	}
106	
107	/* Set zskiplistNodeInfo in node (stored in level[0].span) */
108	static inline void zslSetNodeInfo(zskiplistNode *node, uint8_t levels, uint16_t sdsoffset) {
109	    union {
110	        zskiplistNodeInfo info;
111	        unsigned long span;
112	    } u = { .info = { .levels = levels, .sdsoffset = sdsoffset } };
113	    node->level[0].span = u.span;
114	}
115	
116	/* Compare {score, ele} with node. Returns: 1=bigger 0=equal -1=smaller
117	 *
118	 * Ordering is by score first, then lexicographically by element.
119	 * NULL is treated as +infinity (comes after any real node). */
120	int zslCompareWithNode(double score, sds ele, const zskiplistNode *n) {
121	    if (/*score < */ n == NULL) return -1; /* NULL is +infinity, comes after any real node */
122	    if (score < n->score) return -1;
123	    if (score > n->score) return 1;
124	    /* Scores are equal, compare elements lexicographically */
125	    return sdscmp(ele, zslGetNodeElement(n));
126	}
127	
128	/* Get embedded sds from node. Uses the stored offset to directly access the sds data */
129	sds zslGetNodeElement(const zskiplistNode *node) {
130	    zskiplistNodeInfo *info = zslGetNodeInfo(node);
131	    debugServerAssert(info->sdsoffset != ZSL_OFFSET_NO_ELE);
132	    return (char*)node + info->sdsoffset;
133	}
134	
135	/* Wrapper for dict getKeyId callback - extracts sds from node pointer.
136	 * This allows the dict to store zskiplistNode* but look them up using sds. */
137	const void *zslGetNodeElementForDict(const void *node) {
138	    return zslGetNodeElement((zskiplistNode*)node);
139	}
140	
141	/* Create a skiplist header node with ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL levels */
142	static zskiplistNode *zslCreateHeaderNode(zskiplist *zsl) {
143	    size_t usable;
144	    zskiplistNode *zn = zmalloc_usable(sizeof(*zn) + ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL * sizeof(struct zskiplistLevel), &usable);
145	
146	    /* Initialize all fields */
147	    zn->score = 0;
148	    zn->backward = NULL;
149	
150	    /* Initialize all level pointers and spans */
151	    for (int j = 0; j < ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL; j++) {
152	        zn->level[j].forward = NULL;
153	        zn->level[j].span = 0;  /* Will be overwritten for level[0] below */
154	    }
155	
156	    /* Use ZSL_OFFSET_NO_ELE as sentinel to indicate no embedded sds (header node) */
157	    zslSetNodeInfo(zn, ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL, ZSL_OFFSET_NO_ELE);
158	
159	    /* Track allocation size */
160	    zsl->alloc_size += usable;
161	
162	    return zn;
163	}
164	
165	/* Create a skiplist node with the specified number of levels.
166	 * The SDS string 'ele' is COPIED into an embedded sds within the node allocation.
167	 * This creates a single allocation containing: node + level[] + embedded sds.
168	 * The caller is responsible for freeing 'ele' if it's no longer needed. */
169	static zskiplistNode *zslCreateNode(zskiplist *zsl, int level, double score, sds ele) {
170	    size_t usable;
171	    size_t ele_len = sdslen(ele);
172	    char sds_type = sdsReqType(ele_len);
173	    size_t sds_hdr_len = sdsHdrSize(sds_type);
174	
175	    /* Calculate total size: node fixed part + level[] + sds buffer space */
176	    size_t node_size = sizeof(zskiplistNode) + level * sizeof(struct zskiplistLevel);
177	    size_t sds_buf_size = sds_hdr_len + ele_len + 1;  /* header + data + null terminator */
178	    size_t total_size = node_size + sds_buf_size;
179	
180	    /* Allocate single block for everything */
181	    zskiplistNode *zn = zmalloc_usable(total_size, &usable);
182	
183	    /* Initialize node fields */
184	    zn->score = score;
185	    zn->backward = NULL;
186	
187	    /* Calculate offset from node start to sds data (after sds header) */
188	    size_t sds_offset = node_size + sds_hdr_len;
189	    debugServerAssert(sds_offset < ZSL_OFFSET_MAX_ELE);
190	
191	    /* Initialize embedded sds using sdsnewplacement */
192	    char *sds_buf = (char*)zn + node_size;
193	    sds embedded_sds = sdsnewplacement(sds_buf, sds_buf_size, sds_type, ele, ele_len);
194	
195	    /* Store node info in level[0].span */
196	    zslSetNodeInfo(zn, level, sds_offset);
197	
198	    /* Verify that embedded_sds matches our calculated offset */
199	    serverAssert(embedded_sds == (sds)((char*)zn + sds_offset));
200	
201	    /* Update allocation size tracking */
202	    zsl->alloc_size += usable;
203	
204	    return zn;
205	}
206	
207	/* Create a new skiplist. */
208	zskiplist *zslCreate(void) {
209	    zskiplist *zsl;
210	    size_t zsl_size;
211	
212	    zsl = zmalloc_usable(sizeof(*zsl), &zsl_size);
213	    zsl->level = 1;
214	    zsl->length = 0;
215	    zsl->alloc_size = zsl_size;
216	    zsl->header = zslCreateHeaderNode(zsl);
217	    zsl->header->backward = NULL;
218	    zsl->tail = NULL;
219	    return zsl;
220	}
221	
222	/* Free the specified skiplist node. The embedded SDS is freed as part of
223	 * the single allocation (node + level[] + embedded sds). */
224	static void zslFreeNode(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *node) {
225	    size_t usable;
226	    /* No separate sdsfree() needed - embedded sds is part of node allocation */
227	    zfree_usable(node, &usable);
228	    zsl->alloc_size -= usable;
229	}
230	
231	/* Free a whole skiplist. */
232	void zslFree(zskiplist *zsl) {
233	    zskiplistNode *node = zsl->header->level[0].forward, *next;
234	    size_t usable;
235	
236	    zfree_usable(zsl->header, &usable);
237	    zsl->alloc_size -= usable;
238	    while(node) {
239	        next = node->level[0].forward;
240	        zslFreeNode(zsl, node);
241	        node = next;
242	    }
243	    debugServerAssert(zsl->alloc_size == zmalloc_usable_size(zsl));
244	    zfree(zsl);
245	}
246	
247	/* Return cached total memory used (in bytes) */
248	size_t zslAllocSize(const zskiplist *zsl) { return zsl->alloc_size; }
249	
250	/* Returns a random level for the new skiplist node we are going to create.
251	 * The return value of this function is between 1 and ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL
252	 * (both inclusive), with a powerlaw-alike distribution where higher
253	 * levels are less likely to be returned. */
254	static int zslRandomLevel(void) {
255	    static const int threshold = ZSKIPLIST_P*RAND_MAX;
256	    int level = 1;
257	    while (random() < threshold)
258	        level += 1;
259	    return (level<ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL) ? level : ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL;
260	}
261	
262	/* Insert an already-created node, with its score, element set into the skiplist 
263	 * at the correct position. Updates all forward/backward pointers and spans.
264	 * The node's level must already be set via zslSetNodeInfo(). */
265	static void zslInsertNode(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *node) {
266	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL];  /* Nodes that will point to the new node at each level */
267	    unsigned long rank[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL];     /* Rank (0-based) at each level during traversal */
268	    zskiplistNode *x;
269	    int i, level;
270	    double score = node->score;
271	    sds ele = zslGetNodeElement(node);
272	    level = zslGetNodeInfo(node)->levels;
273	    serverAssert(!isnan(score));
274	
275	    /* Find the position where this node should be inserted */
276	    x = zsl->header;
277	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
278	        /* store rank that is crossed to reach the insert position */
279	        rank[i] = i == (zsl->level-1) ? 0 : rank[i+1];
280	        while (zslCompareWithNode(score, ele, x->level[i].forward) > 0) {
281	            rank[i] += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
282	            x = x->level[i].forward;
283	        }
284	        update[i] = x;
285	    }
286	
287	    /* Update skiplist level if needed */
288	    if (level > zsl->level) {
289	        for (i = zsl->level; i < level; i++) {
290	            rank[i] = 0;
291	            update[i] = zsl->header;
292	            zslSetNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i, zsl->length);
293	        }
294	        zsl->level = level;
295	        zslGetNodeInfo(zsl->header)->levels = level;
296	    }
297	
298	    /* Insert the node at the found position */
299	    for (i = 0; i < level; i++) {
300	        node->level[i].forward = update[i]->level[i].forward;
301	        update[i]->level[i].forward = node;
302	
303	        /* update span covered by update[i] as node is inserted here */
304	        zslSetNodeSpanAtLevel(node, i, zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i) - (rank[0] - rank[i]));
305	        zslSetNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i, (rank[0] - rank[i]) + 1);
306	    }
307	
308	    /* increment span for untouched levels */
309	    for (i = level; i < zsl->level; i++) {
310	        zslIncrNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i, 1);
311	    }
312	
313	    /* Update backward pointers */
314	    node->backward = (update[0] == zsl->header) ? NULL : update[0];
315	    if (node->level[0].forward)
316	        node->level[0].forward->backward = node;
317	    else
318	        zsl->tail = node;
319	
320	    zsl->length++;
321	}
322	
323	/* Insert a new node in the skiplist. Assumes the element does not already
324	 * exist (up to the caller to enforce that). The element 'ele' is COPIED
325	 * into the new node, so the caller retains ownership and can free it. */
326	zskiplistNode *zslInsert(zskiplist *zsl, double score, sds ele) {
327	    int level;
328	
329	    serverAssert(!isnan(score));
330	
331	    /* we assume the element is not already inside, since we allow duplicated
332	     * scores, reinserting the same element should never happen since the
333	     * caller of zslInsert() should test in the hash table if the element is
334	     * already inside or not. */
335	    level = zslRandomLevel();
336	    zskiplistNode *node = zslCreateNode(zsl, level, score, ele);
337	    zslInsertNode(zsl, node);
338	    return node;
339	}
340	
341	/* Internal function used by zslDelete, zslDeleteRangeByScore and
342	 * zslDeleteRangeByRank.
343	 * This function only unlinks the node from the skiplist structure but does NOT free it.
344	 * The caller is responsible for freeing the node with zslFreeNode(). */
345	static void zslUnlinkNode(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *x, zskiplistNode **update) {
346	    int i;
347	    for (i = 0; i < zsl->level; i++) {
348	        if (update[i]->level[i].forward == x) {
349	            zslIncrNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i, zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i) - 1);
350	            update[i]->level[i].forward = x->level[i].forward;
351	        } else {
352	            zslDecrNodeSpanAtLevel(update[i], i, 1);
353	        }
354	    }
355	    if (x->level[0].forward) {
356	        x->level[0].forward->backward = x->backward;
357	    } else {
358	        zsl->tail = x->backward;
359	    }
360	    /* Decrease skiplist level if top levels are empty, and clear their spans */
361	    while(zsl->level > 1 && zsl->header->level[zsl->level-1].forward == NULL) {
362	        zsl->header->level[zsl->level-1].span = 0;
363	        zsl->level--;
364	    }
365	    zsl->length--;
366	}
367	
368	/* Delete the specified node from the skiplist.
369	 * The node is unlinked from all levels and then freed by zslFreeNode(),
370	 * which also frees the embedded SDS string. */
371	static void zslDelete(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *node) {
372	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL], *x;
373	    int i;
374	    double score = node->score;
375	    sds ele = zslGetNodeElement(node);
376	
377	    x = zsl->header;
378	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
379	        while (zslCompareWithNode(score, ele, x->level[i].forward) > 0) {
380	            x = x->level[i].forward;
381	        }
382	        update[i] = x;
383	    }
384	
385	    /* Verify we truly found the node */
386	    serverAssert(x->level[0].forward == node);
387	
388	    zslUnlinkNode(zsl, node, update);
389	    zslFreeNode(zsl, node);
390	}
391	
392	/* Update the score of an element inside the sorted set skiplist.
393	 * If the new score would keep the node in its current position, updates in-place and returns NULL.
394	 * Otherwise, unlinks the node, updates score, reinserts at correct position, and returns node.
395	 * Anyway, the node pointer stays the same (no dict update needed). */
396	static void zslUpdateScore(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *node, double newscore) {
397	    /* Fast path: if the node, after the score update, would be still exactly
398	     * at the same position, we can just update the score without
399	     * actually removing and re-inserting the element in the skiplist. */
400	    if ((node->backward == NULL || node->backward->score < newscore) &&
401	        (node->level[0].forward == NULL || node->level[0].forward->score > newscore))
402	    {
403	        node->score = newscore;
404	        return;
405	    }
406	
407	    /* Slow path: need to reposition the node.
408	     * Find the update[] array for unlinking. */
409	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL], *x;
410	    int i;
411	    double curscore = node->score;
412	    sds ele = zslGetNodeElement(node);
413	
414	    x = zsl->header;
415	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
416	        while (zslCompareWithNode(curscore, ele, x->level[i].forward) > 0) {
417	            x = x->level[i].forward;
418	        }
419	        update[i] = x;
420	    }
421	
422	    /* Verify we found the right node */
423	    serverAssert(x->level[0].forward == node);
424	
425	    /* Unlink, update score, and reinsert at new position.
426	     * We reuse the same node to avoid dict updates. */
427	    zslUnlinkNode(zsl, node, update);
428	    node->score = newscore;
429	    zslInsertNode(zsl, node);
430	}
431	
432	int zslValueGteMin(double value, zrangespec *spec) {
433	    return spec->minex ? (value > spec->min) : (value >= spec->min);
434	}
435	
436	int zslValueLteMax(double value, zrangespec *spec) {
437	    return spec->maxex ? (value < spec->max) : (value <= spec->max);
438	}
439	
440	/* Returns if there is a part of the zset is in range. */
441	static int zslIsInRange(zskiplist *zsl, zrangespec *range) {
442	    zskiplistNode *x;
443	
444	    /* Test for ranges that will always be empty. */
445	    if (range->min > range->max ||
446	            (range->min == range->max && (range->minex || range->maxex)))
447	        return 0;
448	    x = zsl->tail;
449	    if (x == NULL || !zslValueGteMin(x->score,range))
450	        return 0;
451	    x = zsl->header->level[0].forward;
452	    if (x == NULL || !zslValueLteMax(x->score,range))
453	        return 0;
454	    return 1;
455	}
456	
457	/* Find the Nth element within the specified score range.
458	 *
459	 * Parameters:
460	 *   - N is 0-based for forward direction (0 = first element in range)
461	 *   - N can be negative for reverse direction (-1 = last element in range)
462	 *
463	 * Returns:
464	 *   - The skiplist node at position N within the range, or NULL if:
465	 *     * N is out of bounds for the range
466	 *     * The range contains no elements
467	 *   - If out_rank!=NULL, it receives the 1-based absolute rank of the returned node 
468	 */
469	zskiplistNode *zslNthInRange(zskiplist *zsl, zrangespec *range, long n, unsigned long *out_rank) {
470	    zskiplistNode *x;
471	    int i;
472	    long edge_rank = 0; /* 0-based rank of the last element smaller than the range. */
473	    long last_highest_level_rank = 0;
474	    zskiplistNode *last_highest_level_node = NULL;
475	    unsigned long rank_diff;
476	
477	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
478	    if (!zslIsInRange(zsl,range)) return NULL;
479	
480	    /* Go forward while *OUT* of range at level of zsl->level-1. */
481	    x = zsl->header;
482	    i = zsl->level - 1;
483	    while (x->level[i].forward && !zslValueGteMin(x->level[i].forward->score, range)) {
484	        edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
485	        x = x->level[i].forward;
486	    }
487	    /* Remember the last node which has zsl->level-1 levels and its rank. */
488	    last_highest_level_node = x;
489	    last_highest_level_rank = edge_rank;
490	
491	    if (n >= 0) {
492	        for (i = zsl->level - 2; i >= 0; i--) {
493	            /* Go forward while *OUT* of range. */
494	            while (x->level[i].forward && !zslValueGteMin(x->level[i].forward->score, range)) {
495	                /* Count the rank of the last element smaller than the range. */
496	                edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
497	                x = x->level[i].forward;
498	            }
499	        }
500	        /* Check if zsl is long enough. */
501	        if ((unsigned long)(edge_rank + n) >= zsl->length) return NULL;
502	        if (n < ZSKIPLIST_MAX_SEARCH) {
503	            /* If offset is small, we can just jump node by node */
504	            /* rank+1 is the first element in range, so we need n+1 steps to reach target. */
505	            for (i = 0; i < n + 1; i++) { 
506	                x = x->level[0].forward;
507	            }
508	        } else {
509	            /* If offset is big, we can jump from the last zsl->level-1 node. */
510	            rank_diff = edge_rank + 1 + n - last_highest_level_rank;
511	            x = zslGetElementByRankFromNode(last_highest_level_node, zsl->level - 1, rank_diff);
512	        }
513	        /* Check if score <= max. */
514	        if (x && !zslValueLteMax(x->score,range)) return NULL;
515	        /* Store rank if requested. For n >= 0, the returned node is at rank edge_rank + n + 1. */
516	        if (x && out_rank) *out_rank = edge_rank + n + 1;
517	    } else  {
518	        for (i = zsl->level - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
519	            /* Go forward while *IN* range. */
520	            while (x->level[i].forward && zslValueLteMax(x->level[i].forward->score, range)) {
521	                /* Count the rank of the last element in range. */
522	                edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
523	                x = x->level[i].forward;
524	            }
525	        }
526	        /* Check if the range is big enough. */
527	        if (edge_rank < -n) return NULL;
528	        if (n + 1 > -ZSKIPLIST_MAX_SEARCH) {
529	            /* If offset is small, we can just jump node by node */
530	            /* rank is the -1th element in range, so we need -n-1 steps to reach target. */
531	            for (i = 0; i < -n - 1; i++) {
532	                x = x->backward;
533	            }
534	        } else {
535	            /* If offset is big, we can jump from the last zsl->level-1 node. */
536	            /* rank is the last element in range, n is -1-based, so we need n+1 to count backwards. */
537	            rank_diff = edge_rank + 1 + n - last_highest_level_rank;
538	            x = zslGetElementByRankFromNode(last_highest_level_node, zsl->level - 1, rank_diff);
539	        }
540	        /* Check if score >= min. */
541	        if (x && !zslValueGteMin(x->score, range)) return NULL;
542	        /* Store rank if requested. For n < 0, the returned node is at rank edge_rank + n + 1. */
543	        if (x && out_rank) *out_rank = edge_rank + n + 1;
544	    }
545	
546	    return x;
547	}
548	
549	/* Delete all the elements with score between min and max from the skiplist.
550	 * Both min and max can be inclusive or exclusive (see range->minex and
551	 * range->maxex). When inclusive a score >= min && score <= max is deleted.
552	 * Note that this function takes the reference to the hash table view of the
553	 * sorted set, in order to remove the elements from the hash table too. */
554	static unsigned long zslDeleteRangeByScore(zskiplist *zsl, zrangespec *range, dict *dict) {
555	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL], *x;
556	    unsigned long removed = 0;
557	    int i;
558	
559	    x = zsl->header;
560	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
561	        while (x->level[i].forward &&
562	            !zslValueGteMin(x->level[i].forward->score, range))
563	                x = x->level[i].forward;
564	        update[i] = x;
565	    }
566	
567	    /* Current node is the last with score < or <= min. */
568	    x = x->level[0].forward;
569	
570	    /* Delete nodes while in range. */
571	    while (x && zslValueLteMax(x->score, range)) {
572	        zskiplistNode *next = x->level[0].forward;
573	        zslUnlinkNode(zsl,x,update);
574	        dictDelete(dict,zslGetNodeElement(x));
575	        zslFreeNode(zsl, x); /* Here is where x->ele is actually released. */
576	        removed++;
577	        x = next;
578	    }
579	    return removed;
580	}
581	
582	static unsigned long zslDeleteRangeByLex(zskiplist *zsl, zlexrangespec *range, dict *dict) {
583	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL], *x;
584	    unsigned long removed = 0;
585	    int i;
586	
587	
588	    x = zsl->header;
589	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
590	        while (x->level[i].forward &&
591	            !zslLexValueGteMin(zslGetNodeElement(x->level[i].forward),range))
592	                x = x->level[i].forward;
593	        update[i] = x;
594	    }
595	
596	    /* Current node is the last with score < or <= min. */
597	    x = x->level[0].forward;
598	
599	    /* Delete nodes while in range. */
600	    while (x && zslLexValueLteMax(zslGetNodeElement(x),range)) {
601	        zskiplistNode *next = x->level[0].forward;
602	        zslUnlinkNode(zsl,x,update);
603	        dictDelete(dict,zslGetNodeElement(x));
604	        zslFreeNode(zsl, x); /* Here is where x->ele is actually released. */
605	        removed++;
606	        x = next;
607	    }
608	    return removed;
609	}
610	
611	/* Delete all the elements with rank between start and end from the skiplist.
612	 * Start and end are inclusive. Note that start and end need to be 1-based */
613	static unsigned long zslDeleteRangeByRank(zskiplist *zsl, unsigned int start, unsigned int end, dict *dict) {
614	    zskiplistNode *update[ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL], *x;
615	    unsigned long traversed = 0, removed = 0;
616	    int i;
617	
618	    x = zsl->header;
619	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
620	        while (x->level[i].forward && (traversed + zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i)) < start) {
621	            traversed += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
622	            x = x->level[i].forward;
623	        }
624	        update[i] = x;
625	    }
626	
627	    traversed++;
628	    x = x->level[0].forward;
629	    while (x && traversed <= end) {
630	        zskiplistNode *next = x->level[0].forward;
631	        zslUnlinkNode(zsl,x,update);
632	        dictDelete(dict,zslGetNodeElement(x));
633	        zslFreeNode(zsl, x);
634	        removed++;
635	        traversed++;
636	        x = next;
637	    }
638	    return removed;
639	}
640	
641	/* Find the rank for an element by both score and key.
642	 * Returns 0 when the element cannot be found, rank otherwise.
643	 * Note that the rank is 1-based due to the span of zsl->header to the
644	 * first element. */
645	unsigned long zslGetRank(zskiplist *zsl, double score, sds ele) {
646	    zskiplistNode *x;
647	    unsigned long rank = 0;
648	    int i;
649	
650	    x = zsl->header;
651	    for (i = zsl->level-1; i >= 0; i--) {
652	        while (zslCompareWithNode(score, ele, x->level[i].forward) >= 0) {
653	            rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
654	            x = x->level[i].forward;
655	        }
656	
657	        if (x != zsl->header && zslCompareWithNode(score, ele, x) == 0) {
658	            return rank;
659	        }
660	    }
661	    return 0;
662	}
663	
664	/* Find the rank for a skiplist node by walking forward from the node to the end.
665	 * This avoids expensive string comparisons during traversal. The algorithm:
666	 * 1. Start at the given node's top level
667	 * 2. Walk forward to the tail, jumping at each node's top level
668	 * 3. Sum the spans to get distance from node to end
669	 * 4. Calculate rank as (list_length - distance_to_end)
670	 * Time complexity: O(log N) on average, same as traditional approach but faster
671	 * due to avoiding string comparisons. */
672	unsigned long zslGetRankByNode(zskiplist *zsl, zskiplistNode *x) {
673	    unsigned long distance_to_end = 0;
674	    int level;
675	    
676	    /* Walk forward from x to the end, using top level of each node for fast jumps */
677	    while (x) {
678	        level = zslGetNodeInfo(x)->levels - 1;
679	        distance_to_end += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, level);
680	        x = x->level[level].forward;
681	    }
682	    
683	    /* Rank = total nodes - nodes after this one */
684	    return zsl->length - distance_to_end;
685	}
686	
687	/* Finds an element by its rank from start node. The rank argument needs to be 1-based. */
688	static zskiplistNode *zslGetElementByRankFromNode(zskiplistNode *start_node, int start_level, unsigned long rank) {
689	    zskiplistNode *x;
690	    unsigned long traversed = 0;
691	    int i;
692	
693	    x = start_node;
694	    for (i = start_level; i >= 0; i--) {
695	        while (x->level[i].forward && (traversed + zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i)) <= rank)
696	        {
697	            traversed += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
698	            x = x->level[i].forward;
699	        }
700	        if (traversed == rank) {
701	            return x;
702	        }
703	    }
704	    return NULL;
705	}
706	
707	/* Finds an element by its rank. The rank argument needs to be 1-based. */
708	zskiplistNode *zslGetElementByRank(zskiplist *zsl, unsigned long rank) {
709	    return zslGetElementByRankFromNode(zsl->header, zsl->level - 1, rank);
710	}
711	
712	/* Populate the rangespec according to the objects min and max. */
713	static int zslParseRange(robj *min, robj *max, zrangespec *spec) {
714	    char *eptr;
715	    spec->minex = spec->maxex = 0;
716	
717	    /* Parse the min-max interval. If one of the values is prefixed
718	     * by the "(" character, it's considered "open". For instance
719	     * ZRANGEBYSCORE zset (1.5 (2.5 will match min < x < max
720	     * ZRANGEBYSCORE zset 1.5 2.5 will instead match min <= x <= max */
721	    if (min->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INT) {
722	        spec->min = (long)min->ptr;
723	    } else {
724	        size_t len = sdslen(min->ptr);
725	        if (((char*)min->ptr)[0] == '(') {
726	            spec->min = fast_float_strtod((char*)min->ptr+1,len-1,&eptr);
727	            if (eptr[0] != '\0' || isnan(spec->min)) return C_ERR;
728	            spec->minex = 1;
729	        } else {
730	            spec->min = fast_float_strtod((char*)min->ptr,len,&eptr);
731	            if (eptr[0] != '\0' || isnan(spec->min)) return C_ERR;
732	        }
733	    }
734	    if (max->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INT) {
735	        spec->max = (long)max->ptr;
736	    } else {
737	        size_t len = sdslen(max->ptr);
738	        if (((char*)max->ptr)[0] == '(') {
739	            spec->max = fast_float_strtod((char*)max->ptr+1,len-1,&eptr);
740	            if (eptr[0] != '\0' || isnan(spec->max)) return C_ERR;
741	            spec->maxex = 1;
742	        } else {
743	            spec->max = fast_float_strtod((char*)max->ptr,len,&eptr);
744	            if (eptr[0] != '\0' || isnan(spec->max)) return C_ERR;
745	        }
746	    }
747	
748	    return C_OK;
749	}
750	
751	/* ------------------------ Lexicographic ranges ---------------------------- */
752	
753	/* Parse max or min argument of ZRANGEBYLEX.
754	  * (foo means foo (open interval)
755	  * [foo means foo (closed interval)
756	  * - means the min string possible
757	  * + means the max string possible
758	  *
759	  * If the string is valid the *dest pointer is set to the redis object
760	  * that will be used for the comparison, and ex will be set to 0 or 1
761	  * respectively if the item is exclusive or inclusive. C_OK will be
762	  * returned.
763	  *
764	  * If the string is not a valid range C_ERR is returned, and the value
765	  * of *dest and *ex is undefined. */
766	static int zslParseLexRangeItem(robj *item, sds *dest, int *ex) {
767	    char *c = item->ptr;
768	
769	    switch(c[0]) {
770	    case '+':
771	        if (c[1] != '\0') return C_ERR;
772	        *ex = 1;
773	        *dest = shared.maxstring;
774	        return C_OK;
775	    case '-':
776	        if (c[1] != '\0') return C_ERR;
777	        *ex = 1;
778	        *dest = shared.minstring;
779	        return C_OK;
780	    case '(':
781	        *ex = 1;
782	        *dest = sdsnewlen(c+1,sdslen(c)-1);
783	        return C_OK;
784	    case '[':
785	        *ex = 0;
786	        *dest = sdsnewlen(c+1,sdslen(c)-1);
787	        return C_OK;
788	    default:
789	        return C_ERR;
790	    }
791	}
792	
793	/* Free a lex range structure, must be called only after zslParseLexRange()
794	 * populated the structure with success (C_OK returned). */
795	void zslFreeLexRange(zlexrangespec *spec) {
796	    if (spec->min != shared.minstring &&
797	        spec->min != shared.maxstring) sdsfree(spec->min);
798	    if (spec->max != shared.minstring &&
799	        spec->max != shared.maxstring) sdsfree(spec->max);
800	}
801	
802	/* Populate the lex rangespec according to the objects min and max.
803	 *
804	 * Return C_OK on success. On error C_ERR is returned.
805	 * When OK is returned the structure must be freed with zslFreeLexRange(),
806	 * otherwise no release is needed. */
807	int zslParseLexRange(robj *min, robj *max, zlexrangespec *spec) {
808	    /* The range can't be valid if objects are integer encoded.
809	     * Every item must start with ( or [. */
810	    if (min->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INT ||
811	        max->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_INT) return C_ERR;
812	
813	    spec->min = spec->max = NULL;
814	    if (zslParseLexRangeItem(min, &spec->min, &spec->minex) == C_ERR ||
815	        zslParseLexRangeItem(max, &spec->max, &spec->maxex) == C_ERR) {
816	        zslFreeLexRange(spec);
817	        return C_ERR;
818	    } else {
819	        return C_OK;
820	    }
821	}
822	
823	/* This is just a wrapper to sdscmp() that is able to
824	 * handle shared.minstring and shared.maxstring as the equivalent of
825	 * -inf and +inf for strings */
826	static int sdscmplex(sds a, sds b) {
827	    if (a == b) return 0;
828	    if (a == shared.minstring || b == shared.maxstring) return -1;
829	    if (a == shared.maxstring || b == shared.minstring) return 1;
830	    return sdscmp(a,b);
831	}
832	
833	int zslLexValueGteMin(sds value, zlexrangespec *spec) {
834	    return spec->minex ?
835	        (sdscmplex(value,spec->min) > 0) :
836	        (sdscmplex(value,spec->min) >= 0);
837	}
838	
839	int zslLexValueLteMax(sds value, zlexrangespec *spec) {
840	    return spec->maxex ?
841	        (sdscmplex(value,spec->max) < 0) :
842	        (sdscmplex(value,spec->max) <= 0);
843	}
844	
845	/* Returns if there is a part of the zset is in the lex range. */
846	static int zslIsInLexRange(zskiplist *zsl, zlexrangespec *range) {
847	    zskiplistNode *x;
848	
849	    /* Test for ranges that will always be empty. */
850	    int cmp = sdscmplex(range->min,range->max);
851	    if (cmp > 0 || (cmp == 0 && (range->minex || range->maxex)))
852	        return 0;
853	    x = zsl->tail;
854	    if ((x == NULL) || (!zslLexValueGteMin(zslGetNodeElement(x),range)))
855	        return 0;
856	    x = zsl->header->level[0].forward;
857	    if ((x == NULL) || (!zslLexValueLteMax(zslGetNodeElement(x),range)))
858	        return 0;
859	    return 1;
860	}
861	
862	/* Find the Nth node that is contained in the specified range. N should be 0-based.
863	 * Negative N works for reversed order (-1 represents the last element). Returns
864	 * NULL when no element is contained in the range.
865	 * If out_rank is not NULL, stores the 1-based rank of the returned node. */
866	zskiplistNode *zslNthInLexRange(zskiplist *zsl, zlexrangespec *range, long n, unsigned long *out_rank) {
867	    zskiplistNode *x;
868	    int i;
869	    long edge_rank = 0;
870	    long last_highest_level_rank = 0;
871	    zskiplistNode *last_highest_level_node = NULL;
872	    unsigned long rank_diff;
873	
874	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
875	    if (!zslIsInLexRange(zsl,range)) return NULL;
876	
877	    /* Go forward while *OUT* of range at level of zsl->level-1. */
878	    x = zsl->header;
879	    i = zsl->level - 1;
880	    while (x->level[i].forward && !zslLexValueGteMin(zslGetNodeElement(x->level[i].forward), range)) {
881	        edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
882	        x = x->level[i].forward;
883	    }
884	    /* Remember the last node which has zsl->level-1 levels and its rank. */
885	    last_highest_level_node = x;
886	    last_highest_level_rank = edge_rank;
887	
888	    if (n >= 0) {
889	        for (i = zsl->level - 2; i >= 0; i--) {
890	            /* Go forward while *OUT* of range. */
891	            while (x->level[i].forward && !zslLexValueGteMin(zslGetNodeElement(x->level[i].forward), range)) {
892	                /* Count the rank of the last element smaller than the range. */
893	                edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
894	                x = x->level[i].forward;
895	            }
896	        }
897	        /* Check if zsl is long enough. */
898	        if ((unsigned long)(edge_rank + n) >= zsl->length) return NULL; 
899	        if (n < ZSKIPLIST_MAX_SEARCH) {
900	            /* If offset is small, we can just jump node by node */
901	            /* rank+1 is the first element in range, so we need n+1 steps to reach target. */
902	            for (i = 0; i < n + 1; i++) { 
903	                x = x->level[0].forward;
904	            }
905	        } else {
906	            /* If offset is big, we can jump from the last zsl->level-1 node. */
907	            rank_diff = edge_rank + 1 + n - last_highest_level_rank;
908	            x = zslGetElementByRankFromNode(last_highest_level_node, zsl->level - 1, rank_diff);
909	        }
910	        /* Check if score <= max. */
911	        if (x && !zslLexValueLteMax(zslGetNodeElement(x),range)) return NULL;
912	        /* Store rank if requested. For n >= 0, the returned node is at rank edge_rank + n + 1. */
913	        if (x && out_rank) *out_rank = edge_rank + n + 1;
914	    } else {
915	        for (i = zsl->level - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
916	            /* Go forward while *IN* range. */
917	            while (x->level[i].forward && zslLexValueLteMax(zslGetNodeElement(x->level[i].forward), range)) {
918	                /* Count the rank of the last element in range. */
919	                edge_rank += zslGetNodeSpanAtLevel(x, i);
920	                x = x->level[i].forward;
921	            }
922	        }
923	        /* Check if the range is big enough. */
924	        if (edge_rank < -n) return NULL;
925	        if (n + 1 > -ZSKIPLIST_MAX_SEARCH) {
926	            /* If offset is small, we can just jump node by node */
927	            for (i = 0; i < -n - 1; i++) {
928	                x = x->backward;
929	            }
930	        } else {
931	            /* If offset is big, we can jump from the last zsl->level-1 node. */
932	            /* rank is the last element in range, n is -1-based, so we need n+1 to count backwards. */
933	            rank_diff = edge_rank + 1 + n - last_highest_level_rank;
934	            x = zslGetElementByRankFromNode(last_highest_level_node, zsl->level - 1, rank_diff);
935	        }
936	        /* Check if score >= min. */
937	        if (x && !zslLexValueGteMin(zslGetNodeElement(x), range)) return NULL;
938	        /* Store rank if requested. For n < 0, the returned node is at rank edge_rank + n + 1. */
939	        if (x && out_rank) *out_rank = edge_rank + n + 1;
940	    }
941	
942	    return x;
943	}

946	 * Listpack-backed sorted set API

948	
949	static double zzlStrtod(unsigned char *vstr, unsigned int vlen) {
950	    return fast_float_strtod((char*)vstr, vlen, NULL);
951	}
952	
953	double zzlGetScore(unsigned char *sptr) {
954	    unsigned char *vstr;
955	    unsigned int vlen;
956	    long long vlong;
957	    double score;
958	
959	    serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
960	    vstr = lpGetValue(sptr,&vlen,&vlong);
961	
962	    if (vstr) {
963	        score = zzlStrtod(vstr,vlen);
964	    } else {
965	        score = vlong;
966	    }
967	
968	    return score;
969	}

971	/* Return a listpack element as an SDS string. */
972	sds lpGetObject(unsigned char *sptr) {
973	    unsigned char *vstr;
974	    unsigned int vlen;
975	    long long vlong;
976	
977	    serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
978	    vstr = lpGetValue(sptr,&vlen,&vlong);
979	
980	    if (vstr) {
981	        return sdsnewlen((char*)vstr,vlen);
982	    } else {
983	        return sdsfromlonglong(vlong);
984	    }
985	}
986	
987	/* Compare element in sorted set with given element. */
988	static int zzlCompareElements(unsigned char *eptr, unsigned char *cstr, unsigned int clen) {
989	    unsigned char *vstr;
990	    unsigned int vlen;
991	    long long vlong;
992	    unsigned char vbuf[32];
993	    int minlen, cmp;
994	
995	    vstr = lpGetValue(eptr,&vlen,&vlong);
996	    if (vstr == NULL) {
997	        /* Store string representation of long long in buf. */
998	        vlen = ll2string((char*)vbuf,sizeof(vbuf),vlong);
999	        vstr = vbuf;
1000	    }
1001	
1002	    minlen = (vlen < clen) ? vlen : clen;
1003	    cmp = memcmp(vstr,cstr,minlen);
1004	    if (cmp == 0) return vlen-clen;
1005	    return cmp;
1006	}
1007	
1008	static unsigned int zzlLength(unsigned char *zl) {
1009	    return lpLength(zl)/2;
1010	}
1011	
1012	/* Move to next entry based on the values in eptr and sptr. Both are set to
1013	 * NULL when there is no next entry. */
1014	void zzlNext(unsigned char *zl, unsigned char **eptr, unsigned char **sptr) {
1015	    unsigned char *_eptr, *_sptr;
1016	    serverAssert(*eptr != NULL && *sptr != NULL);
1017	
1018	    _eptr = lpNext(zl,*sptr);
1019	    if (_eptr != NULL) {
1020	        _sptr = lpNext(zl,_eptr);
1021	        serverAssert(_sptr != NULL);
1022	    } else {
1023	        /* No next entry. */
1024	        _sptr = NULL;
1025	    }
1026	
1027	    *eptr = _eptr;
1028	    *sptr = _sptr;
1029	}
1030	
1031	/* Move to the previous entry based on the values in eptr and sptr. Both are
1032	 * set to NULL when there is no prev entry. */
1033	void zzlPrev(unsigned char *zl, unsigned char **eptr, unsigned char **sptr) {
1034	    unsigned char *_eptr, *_sptr;
1035	    serverAssert(*eptr != NULL && *sptr != NULL);
1036	
1037	    _sptr = lpPrev(zl,*eptr);
1038	    if (_sptr != NULL) {
1039	        _eptr = lpPrev(zl,_sptr);
1040	        serverAssert(_eptr != NULL);
1041	    } else {
1042	        /* No previous entry. */
1043	        _eptr = NULL;
1044	    }
1045	
1046	    *eptr = _eptr;
1047	    *sptr = _sptr;
1048	}
1049	
1050	/* Returns if there is a part of the zset is in range. Should only be used
1051	 * internally by zzlFirstInRange and zzlLastInRange. */
1052	static int zzlIsInRange(unsigned char *zl, zrangespec *range) {
1053	    unsigned char *p;
1054	    double score;
1055	
1056	    /* Test for ranges that will always be empty. */
1057	    if (range->min > range->max ||
1058	            (range->min == range->max && (range->minex || range->maxex)))
1059	        return 0;
1060	
1061	    p = lpSeek(zl,-1); /* Last score. */
1062	    if (p == NULL) return 0; /* Empty sorted set */
1063	    score = zzlGetScore(p);
1064	    if (!zslValueGteMin(score,range))
1065	        return 0;
1066	
1067	    p = lpSeek(zl,1); /* First score. */
1068	    serverAssert(p != NULL);
1069	    score = zzlGetScore(p);
1070	    if (!zslValueLteMax(score,range))
1071	        return 0;
1072	
1073	    return 1;
1074	}
1075	
1076	/* Find pointer to the first element contained in the specified range.
1077	 * Returns NULL when no element is contained in the range. */
1078	unsigned char *zzlFirstInRange(unsigned char *zl, zrangespec *range) {
1079	    unsigned char *eptr = lpSeek(zl,0), *sptr;
1080	    double score;
1081	
1082	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
1083	    if (!zzlIsInRange(zl,range)) return NULL;
1084	
1085	    while (eptr != NULL) {
1086	        sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr);
1087	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1088	
1089	        score = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1090	        if (zslValueGteMin(score,range)) {
1091	            /* Check if score <= max. */
1092	            if (zslValueLteMax(score,range))
1093	                return eptr;
1094	            return NULL;
1095	        }
1096	
1097	        /* Move to next element. */
1098	        eptr = lpNext(zl,sptr);
1099	    }
1100	
1101	    return NULL;
1102	}
1103	
1104	/* Find pointer to the last element contained in the specified range.
1105	 * Returns NULL when no element is contained in the range. */
1106	unsigned char *zzlLastInRange(unsigned char *zl, zrangespec *range) {
1107	    unsigned char *eptr = lpSeek(zl,-2), *sptr;
1108	    double score;
1109	
1110	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
1111	    if (!zzlIsInRange(zl,range)) return NULL;
1112	
1113	    while (eptr != NULL) {
1114	        sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr);
1115	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1116	
1117	        score = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1118	        if (zslValueLteMax(score,range)) {
1119	            /* Check if score >= min. */
1120	            if (zslValueGteMin(score,range))
1121	                return eptr;
1122	            return NULL;
1123	        }
1124	
1125	        /* Move to previous element by moving to the score of previous element.
1126	         * When this returns NULL, we know there also is no element. */
1127	        sptr = lpPrev(zl,eptr);
1128	        if (sptr != NULL)
1129	            serverAssert((eptr = lpPrev(zl,sptr)) != NULL);
1130	        else
1131	            eptr = NULL;
1132	    }
1133	
1134	    return NULL;
1135	}
1136	
1137	int zzlLexValueGteMin(unsigned char *p, zlexrangespec *spec) {
1138	    sds value = lpGetObject(p);
1139	    int res = zslLexValueGteMin(value,spec);
1140	    sdsfree(value);
1141	    return res;
1142	}
1143	
1144	int zzlLexValueLteMax(unsigned char *p, zlexrangespec *spec) {
1145	    sds value = lpGetObject(p);
1146	    int res = zslLexValueLteMax(value,spec);
1147	    sdsfree(value);
1148	    return res;
1149	}
1150	
1151	/* Returns if there is a part of the zset is in range. Should only be used
1152	 * internally by zzlFirstInLexRange and zzlLastInLexRange. */
1153	static int zzlIsInLexRange(unsigned char *zl, zlexrangespec *range) {
1154	    unsigned char *p;
1155	
1156	    /* Test for ranges that will always be empty. */
1157	    int cmp = sdscmplex(range->min,range->max);
1158	    if (cmp > 0 || (cmp == 0 && (range->minex || range->maxex)))
1159	        return 0;
1160	
1161	    p = lpSeek(zl,-2); /* Last element. */
1162	    if (p == NULL) return 0;
1163	    if (!zzlLexValueGteMin(p,range))
1164	        return 0;
1165	
1166	    p = lpSeek(zl,0); /* First element. */
1167	    serverAssert(p != NULL);
1168	    if (!zzlLexValueLteMax(p,range))
1169	        return 0;
1170	
1171	    return 1;
1172	}
1173	
1174	/* Find pointer to the first element contained in the specified lex range.
1175	 * Returns NULL when no element is contained in the range. */
1176	unsigned char *zzlFirstInLexRange(unsigned char *zl, zlexrangespec *range) {
1177	    unsigned char *eptr = lpSeek(zl,0), *sptr;
1178	
1179	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
1180	    if (!zzlIsInLexRange(zl,range)) return NULL;
1181	
1182	    while (eptr != NULL) {
1183	        if (zzlLexValueGteMin(eptr,range)) {
1184	            /* Check if score <= max. */
1185	            if (zzlLexValueLteMax(eptr,range))
1186	                return eptr;
1187	            return NULL;
1188	        }
1189	
1190	        /* Move to next element. */
1191	        sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr); /* This element score. Skip it. */
1192	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1193	        eptr = lpNext(zl,sptr); /* Next element. */
1194	    }
1195	
1196	    return NULL;
1197	}
1198	
1199	/* Find pointer to the last element contained in the specified lex range.
1200	 * Returns NULL when no element is contained in the range. */
1201	unsigned char *zzlLastInLexRange(unsigned char *zl, zlexrangespec *range) {
1202	    unsigned char *eptr = lpSeek(zl,-2), *sptr;
1203	
1204	    /* If everything is out of range, return early. */
1205	    if (!zzlIsInLexRange(zl,range)) return NULL;
1206	
1207	    while (eptr != NULL) {
1208	        if (zzlLexValueLteMax(eptr,range)) {
1209	            /* Check if score >= min. */
1210	            if (zzlLexValueGteMin(eptr,range))
1211	                return eptr;
1212	            return NULL;
1213	        }
1214	
1215	        /* Move to previous element by moving to the score of previous element.
1216	         * When this returns NULL, we know there also is no element. */
1217	        sptr = lpPrev(zl,eptr);
1218	        if (sptr != NULL)
1219	            serverAssert((eptr = lpPrev(zl,sptr)) != NULL);
1220	        else
1221	            eptr = NULL;
1222	    }
1223	
1224	    return NULL;
1225	}
1226	
1227	static unsigned char *zzlFind(unsigned char *lp, sds ele, double *score) {
1228	    unsigned char *eptr, *sptr;
1229	
1230	    if ((eptr = lpFirst(lp)) == NULL) return NULL;
1231	    eptr = lpFind(lp, eptr, (unsigned char*)ele, sdslen(ele), 1);
1232	    if (eptr) {
1233	        sptr = lpNext(lp,eptr);
1234	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1235	
1236	        /* Matching element, pull out score. */
1237	        if (score != NULL) *score = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1238	        return eptr;
1239	    }
1240	
1241	    return NULL;
1242	}
1243	
1244	/* Delete (element,score) pair from listpack. Use local copy of eptr because we
1245	 * don't want to modify the one given as argument. */
1246	static unsigned char *zzlDelete(unsigned char *zl, unsigned char *eptr) {
1247	    return lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(zl,&eptr,2);
1248	}
1249	
1250	static unsigned char *zzlInsertAt(unsigned char *zl, unsigned char *eptr, sds ele, double score) {
1251	    char scorebuf[MAX_D2STRING_CHARS];
1252	    int scorelen = 0;
1253	    long long lscore;
1254	    int score_is_long = double2ll(score, &lscore);
1255	    if (!score_is_long)
1256	        scorelen = d2string(scorebuf,sizeof(scorebuf),score);
1257	
1258	    listpackEntry entries[2];
1259	    entries[0].sval = (unsigned char*)ele;
1260	    entries[0].slen = sdslen(ele);
1261	    if (score_is_long) {
1262	        entries[1].sval = NULL;
1263	        entries[1].lval = lscore;
1264	    } else {
1265	        entries[1].sval = (unsigned char*)scorebuf;
1266	        entries[1].slen = scorelen;
1267	    }
1268	
1269	    if (eptr == NULL)
1270	        zl = lpBatchAppend(zl, entries, 2);
1271	    else
1272	        zl = lpBatchInsert(zl, eptr, LP_BEFORE, entries, 2, NULL);
1273	
1274	    return zl;
1275	}
1276	
1277	/* Insert (element,score) pair in listpack. This function assumes the element is
1278	 * not yet present in the list. */
1279	unsigned char *zzlInsert(unsigned char *zl, sds ele, double score) {
1280	    unsigned char *eptr = lpSeek(zl,0), *sptr;
1281	    double s;
1282	
1283	    while (eptr != NULL) {
1284	        sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr);
1285	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1286	        s = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1287	
1288	        if (s > score) {
1289	            /* First element with score larger than score for element to be
1290	             * inserted. This means we should take its spot in the list to
1291	             * maintain ordering. */
1292	            zl = zzlInsertAt(zl,eptr,ele,score);
1293	            break;
1294	        } else if (s == score) {
1295	            /* Ensure lexicographical ordering for elements. */
1296	            if (zzlCompareElements(eptr,(unsigned char*)ele,sdslen(ele)) > 0) {
1297	                zl = zzlInsertAt(zl,eptr,ele,score);
1298	                break;
1299	            }
1300	        }
1301	
1302	        /* Move to next element. */
1303	        eptr = lpNext(zl,sptr);
1304	    }
1305	
1306	    /* Push on tail of list when it was not yet inserted. */
1307	    if (eptr == NULL)
1308	        zl = zzlInsertAt(zl,NULL,ele,score);
1309	    return zl;
1310	}
1311	
1312	static unsigned char *zzlDeleteRangeByScore(unsigned char *zl, zrangespec *range, unsigned long *deleted) {
1313	    unsigned char *eptr, *sptr;
1314	    double score;
1315	    unsigned long num = 0;
1316	
1317	    if (deleted != NULL) *deleted = 0;
1318	
1319	    eptr = zzlFirstInRange(zl,range);
1320	    if (eptr == NULL) return zl;
1321	
1322	    /* When the tail of the listpack is deleted, eptr will be NULL. */
1323	    while (eptr && (sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr)) != NULL) {
1324	        score = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1325	        if (zslValueLteMax(score,range)) {
1326	            /* Delete both the element and the score. */
1327	            zl = lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(zl,&eptr,2);
1328	            num++;
1329	        } else {
1330	            /* No longer in range. */
1331	            break;
1332	        }
1333	    }
1334	
1335	    if (deleted != NULL) *deleted = num;
1336	    return zl;
1337	}
1338	
1339	static unsigned char *zzlDeleteRangeByLex(unsigned char *zl, zlexrangespec *range, unsigned long *deleted) {
1340	    unsigned char *eptr, *sptr;
1341	    unsigned long num = 0;
1342	
1343	    if (deleted != NULL) *deleted = 0;
1344	
1345	    eptr = zzlFirstInLexRange(zl,range);
1346	    if (eptr == NULL) return zl;
1347	
1348	    /* When the tail of the listpack is deleted, eptr will be NULL. */
1349	    while (eptr && (sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr)) != NULL) {
1350	        if (zzlLexValueLteMax(eptr,range)) {
1351	            /* Delete both the element and the score. */
1352	            zl = lpDeleteRangeWithEntry(zl,&eptr,2);
1353	            num++;
1354	        } else {
1355	            /* No longer in range. */
1356	            break;
1357	        }
1358	    }
1359	
1360	    if (deleted != NULL) *deleted = num;
1361	    return zl;
1362	}
1363	
1364	/* Delete all the elements with rank between start and end from the skiplist.
1365	 * Start and end are inclusive. Note that start and end need to be 1-based */
1366	static unsigned char *zzlDeleteRangeByRank(unsigned char *zl, unsigned int start, unsigned int end, unsigned long *deleted) {
1367	    unsigned int num = (end-start)+1;
1368	    if (deleted) *deleted = num;
1369	    zl = lpDeleteRange(zl,2*(start-1),2*num);
1370	    return zl;
1371	}

1373	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1374	 * Common sorted set API
1375	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
1376	
1377	unsigned long zsetLength(const robj *zobj) {
1378	    unsigned long length = 0;
1379	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1380	        length = zzlLength(zobj->ptr);
1381	    } else if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1382	        length = ((const zset*)zobj->ptr)->zsl->length;
1383	    } else {
1384	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1385	    }
1386	    return length;
1387	}
1388	
1389	size_t zsetAllocSize(const robj *o) {
1390	    serverAssertWithInfo(NULL,o,o->type == OBJ_ZSET);
1391	    size_t size = 0;
1392	    if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1393	        size = lpBytes(o->ptr);
1394	    } else if (o->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1395	        dict *d = ((zset*)o->ptr)->dict;
1396	        zskiplist *zsl = ((zset*)o->ptr)->zsl;
1397	        size = sizeof(zset) + zslAllocSize(zsl) +
1398	            sizeof(dict) + dictMemUsage(d);
1399	    } else {
1400	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1401	    }
1402	    return size;
1403	}
1404	
1405	/* Factory method to return a zset.
1406	 *
1407	 * The size hint indicates approximately how many items will be added,
1408	 * and the value len hint indicates the approximate individual size of the added elements,
1409	 * they are used to determine the initial representation.
1410	 *
1411	 * If the hints are not known, and underestimation or 0 is suitable. 
1412	 * We should never pass a negative value because it will convert to a very large unsigned number. */
1413	robj *zsetTypeCreate(size_t size_hint, size_t val_len_hint) {
1414	    if (size_hint <= server.zset_max_listpack_entries &&
1415	        val_len_hint <= server.zset_max_listpack_value)
1416	    {
1417	        return createZsetListpackObject();
1418	    }
1419	
1420	    robj *zobj = createZsetObject();
1421	    zset *zs = zobj->ptr;
1422	    dictExpand(zs->dict, size_hint);
1423	    return zobj;
1424	}
1425	
1426	/* Check if the existing zset should be converted to another encoding based off the
1427	 * the size hint. */
1428	void zsetTypeMaybeConvert(robj *zobj, size_t size_hint) {
1429	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK &&
1430	        size_hint > server.zset_max_listpack_entries)
1431	    {
1432	        zsetConvertAndExpand(zobj, OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST, size_hint);
1433	    }
1434	}
1435	
1436	/* Convert the zset to specified encoding. The zset dict (when converting
1437	 * to a skiplist) is presized to hold the number of elements in the original
1438	 * zset. */
1439	void zsetConvert(robj *zobj, int encoding) {
1440	    zsetConvertAndExpand(zobj, encoding, zsetLength(zobj));
1441	}
1442	
1443	/* Converts a zset to the specified encoding, pre-sizing it for 'cap' elements. */
1444	void zsetConvertAndExpand(robj *zobj, int encoding, unsigned long cap) {
1445	    zset *zs;
1446	    zskiplistNode *node, *next;
1447	    sds ele;
1448	    double score;
1449	
1450	    if (zobj->encoding == encoding) return;
1451	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1452	        unsigned char *zl = zobj->ptr;
1453	        unsigned char *eptr, *sptr;
1454	        unsigned char *vstr;
1455	        unsigned int vlen;
1456	        long long vlong;
1457	
1458	        if (encoding != OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST)
1459	            serverPanic("Unknown target encoding");
1460	
1461	        zs = zmalloc(sizeof(*zs));
1462	        zs->dict = dictCreate(&zsetDictType);
1463	        zs->zsl = zslCreate();
1464	
1465	        /* Presize the dict to avoid rehashing */
1466	        dictExpand(zs->dict, cap);
1467	
1468	        eptr = lpSeek(zl,0);
1469	        if (eptr != NULL) {
1470	            sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr);
1471	            serverAssertWithInfo(NULL,zobj,sptr != NULL);
1472	        }
1473	
1474	        while (eptr != NULL) {
1475	            score = zzlGetScore(sptr);
1476	            vstr = lpGetValue(eptr,&vlen,&vlong);
1477	            if (vstr == NULL)
1478	                ele = sdsfromlonglong(vlong);
1479	            else
1480	                ele = sdsnewlen((char*)vstr,vlen);
1481	
1482	            node = zslInsert(zs->zsl,score,ele);
1483	            serverAssert(dictAdd(zs->dict, node, NULL) == DICT_OK);
1484	            sdsfree(ele); /* zslInsert copied it, we can free our copy */
1485	            zzlNext(zl,&eptr,&sptr);
1486	        }
1487	
1488	        zfree(zobj->ptr);
1489	        zobj->ptr = zs;
1490	        zobj->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST;
1491	    } else if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1492	        unsigned char *zl = lpNew(0);
1493	
1494	        if (encoding != OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK)
1495	            serverPanic("Unknown target encoding");
1496	
1497	        /* Approach similar to zslFree(), since we want to free the skiplist at
1498	         * the same time as creating the listpack. */
1499	        zs = zobj->ptr;
1500	        dictRelease(zs->dict);
1501	        node = zs->zsl->header->level[0].forward;
1502	        zfree(zs->zsl->header);
1503	
1504	        while (node) {
1505	            zl = zzlInsertAt(zl,NULL,zslGetNodeElement(node),node->score);
1506	            next = node->level[0].forward;
1507	            zslFreeNode(zs->zsl, node);
1508	            node = next;
1509	        }
1510	
1511	        zfree(zs->zsl);
1512	        zfree(zs);
1513	        zobj->ptr = zl;
1514	        zobj->encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK;
1515	    } else {
1516	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1517	    }
1518	}
1519	
1520	/* Convert the sorted set object into a listpack if it is not already a listpack
1521	 * and if the number of elements and the maximum element size and total elements size
1522	 * are within the expected ranges. */
1523	void zsetConvertToListpackIfNeeded(robj *zobj, size_t maxelelen, size_t totelelen) {
1524	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) return;
1525	    zset *zset = zobj->ptr;
1526	
1527	    if (zset->zsl->length <= server.zset_max_listpack_entries &&
1528	        maxelelen <= server.zset_max_listpack_value &&
1529	        lpSafeToAdd(NULL, totelelen))
1530	    {
1531	        zsetConvert(zobj,OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK);
1532	    }
1533	}
1534	
1535	/* Return (by reference) the score of the specified member of the sorted set
1536	 * storing it into *score. If the element does not exist C_ERR is returned
1537	 * otherwise C_OK is returned and *score is correctly populated.
1538	 * If 'zobj' or 'member' is NULL, C_ERR is returned. */
1539	int zsetScore(robj *zobj, sds member, double *score) {
1540	    if (!zobj || !member) return C_ERR;
1541	
1542	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1543	        if (zzlFind(zobj->ptr, member, score) == NULL) return C_ERR;
1544	    } else if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1545	        zset *zs = zobj->ptr;
1546	        dictEntry *de = dictFind(zs->dict, member);
1547	        if (de == NULL) return C_ERR;
1548	        zskiplistNode *znode = dictGetKey(de);
1549	        *score = znode->score;
1550	    } else {
1551	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1552	    }
1553	    return C_OK;
1554	}
1555	
1556	/* Add a new element or update the score of an existing element in a sorted
1557	 * set, regardless of its encoding.
1558	 *
1559	 * The set of flags change the command behavior. 
1560	 *
1561	 * The input flags are the following:
1562	 *
1563	 * ZADD_INCR: Increment the current element score by 'score' instead of updating
1564	 *            the current element score. If the element does not exist, we
1565	 *            assume 0 as previous score.
1566	 * ZADD_NX:   Perform the operation only if the element does not exist.
1567	 * ZADD_XX:   Perform the operation only if the element already exist.
1568	 * ZADD_GT:   Perform the operation on existing elements only if the new score is 
1569	 *            greater than the current score.
1570	 * ZADD_LT:   Perform the operation on existing elements only if the new score is 
1571	 *            less than the current score.
1572	 *
1573	 * When ZADD_INCR is used, the new score of the element is stored in
1574	 * '*newscore' if 'newscore' is not NULL.
1575	 *
1576	 * The returned flags are the following:
1577	 *
1578	 * ZADD_NAN:     The resulting score is not a number.
1579	 * ZADD_ADDED:   The element was added (not present before the call).
1580	 * ZADD_UPDATED: The element score was updated.
1581	 * ZADD_NOP:     No operation was performed because of NX or XX.
1582	 *
1583	 * Return value:
1584	 *
1585	 * The function returns 1 on success, and sets the appropriate flags
1586	 * ADDED or UPDATED to signal what happened during the operation (note that
1587	 * none could be set if we re-added an element using the same score it used
1588	 * to have, or in the case a zero increment is used).
1589	 *
1590	 * The function returns 0 on error, currently only when the increment
1591	 * produces a NAN condition, or when the 'score' value is NAN since the
1592	 * start.
1593	 *
1594	 * The command as a side effect of adding a new element may convert the sorted
1595	 * set internal encoding from listpack to hashtable+skiplist.
1596	 *
1597	 * Memory management of 'ele':
1598	 *
1599	 * The function does not take ownership of the 'ele' SDS string, but copies
1600	 * it if needed. */
1601	int zsetAdd(robj *zobj, double score, sds ele, int in_flags, int *out_flags, double *newscore) {
1602	    /* Turn options into simple to check vars. */
1603	    int incr = (in_flags & ZADD_IN_INCR) != 0;
1604	    int nx = (in_flags & ZADD_IN_NX) != 0;
1605	    int xx = (in_flags & ZADD_IN_XX) != 0;
1606	    int gt = (in_flags & ZADD_IN_GT) != 0;
1607	    int lt = (in_flags & ZADD_IN_LT) != 0;
1608	    *out_flags = 0; /* We'll return our response flags. */
1609	    double curscore;
1610	
1611	    /* NaN as input is an error regardless of all the other parameters. */
1612	    if (isnan(score)) {
1613	        *out_flags = ZADD_OUT_NAN;
1614	        return 0;
1615	    }
1616	
1617	    /* Update the sorted set according to its encoding. */
1618	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1619	        unsigned char *eptr;
1620	
1621	        if ((eptr = zzlFind(zobj->ptr,ele,&curscore)) != NULL) {
1622	            /* NX? Return, same element already exists. */
1623	            if (nx) {
1624	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1625	                return 1;
1626	            }
1627	
1628	            /* Prepare the score for the increment if needed. */
1629	            if (incr) {
1630	                score += curscore;
1631	                if (isnan(score)) {
1632	                    *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NAN;
1633	                    return 0;
1634	                }
1635	            }
1636	
1637	            /* GT/LT? Only update if score is greater/less than current. */
1638	            if ((lt && score >= curscore) || (gt && score <= curscore)) {
1639	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1640	                return 1;
1641	            }
1642	
1643	            if (newscore) *newscore = score;
1644	
1645	            /* Remove and re-insert when score changed. */
1646	            if (score != curscore) {
1647	                zobj->ptr = zzlDelete(zobj->ptr,eptr);
1648	                zobj->ptr = zzlInsert(zobj->ptr,ele,score);
1649	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_UPDATED;
1650	            }
1651	            return 1;
1652	        } else if (!xx) {
1653	            /* check if the element is too large or the list
1654	             * becomes too long *before* executing zzlInsert. */
1655	            if (zzlLength(zobj->ptr)+1 > server.zset_max_listpack_entries ||
1656	                sdslen(ele) > server.zset_max_listpack_value ||
1657	                !lpSafeToAdd(zobj->ptr, sdslen(ele)))
1658	            {
1659	                zsetConvertAndExpand(zobj, OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST, zsetLength(zobj) + 1);
1660	            } else {
1661	                zobj->ptr = zzlInsert(zobj->ptr,ele,score);
1662	                if (newscore) *newscore = score;
1663	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_ADDED;
1664	                return 1;
1665	            }
1666	        } else {
1667	            *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1668	            return 1;
1669	        }
1670	    }
1671	
1672	    /* Note that the above block handling listpack would have either returned or
1673	     * converted the key to skiplist. */
1674	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1675	        zset *zs = zobj->ptr;
1676	        zskiplistNode *znode;
1677	        dictEntry *de;
1678	        dictEntryLink bucket, link;
1679	
1680	        /* Use dictFindLink to find the element and get the bucket for potential insertion.
1681	         * This avoids a second lookup in dictAdd() if the element doesn't exist. */
1682	        link = dictFindLink(zs->dict, ele, &bucket);
1683	
1684	        if (link != NULL) {
1685	            /* Element exists - get the dictEntry from the link */
1686	            de = *link;
1687	
1688	            /* NX? Return, same element already exists. */
1689	            if (nx) {
1690	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1691	                return 1;
1692	            }
1693	
1694	            /* Get the node pointer from dict entry */
1695	            znode = dictGetKey(de);
1696	            curscore = znode->score;
1697	
1698	            /* Prepare the score for the increment if needed. */
1699	            if (incr) {
1700	                score += curscore;
1701	                if (isnan(score)) {
1702	                    *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NAN;
1703	                    return 0;
1704	                }
1705	            }
1706	
1707	            /* GT/LT? Only update if score is greater/less than current. */
1708	            if ((lt && score >= curscore) || (gt && score <= curscore)) {
1709	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1710	                return 1;
1711	            }
1712	
1713	            if (newscore) *newscore = score;
1714	
1715	            /* Remove and re-insert when score changes. */
1716	            if (score != curscore) {
1717	                zslUpdateScore(zs->zsl, znode, score);
1718	                /* Note that we did not remove the original element from
1719	                 * the hash table representing the sorted set, so we don't
1720	                 * need to update the dict - the node pointer stays the same. */
1721	                *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_UPDATED;
1722	            }
1723	            return 1;
1724	        } else if (!xx) {
1725	            /* Element doesn't exist - create node with embedded sds and add to skiplist */
1726	            znode = zslInsert(zs->zsl, score, ele);
1727	
1728	            /* Add node pointer to dict using the bucket we already found */
1729	            dictSetKeyAtLink(zs->dict, znode, &bucket, 1);
1730	
1731	            *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_ADDED;
1732	            if (newscore) *newscore = score;
1733	            return 1;
1734	        } else {
1735	            *out_flags |= ZADD_OUT_NOP;
1736	            return 1;
1737	        }
1738	    } else {
1739	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1740	    }
1741	    return 0; /* Never reached. */
1742	}
1743	
1744	/* Deletes the element 'ele' from the sorted set encoded as a skiplist+dict,
1745	 * returning 1 if the element existed and was deleted, 0 otherwise (the
1746	 * element was not there). It does not resize the dict after deleting the
1747	 * element. */
1748	static int zsetRemoveFromSkiplist(zset *zs, sds ele) {
1749	    dictEntry *de;
1750	
1751	    de = dictUnlink(zs->dict,ele);
1752	    if (de != NULL) {
1753	        /* Get the node and score in order to delete from the skiplist later. */
1754	        zskiplistNode *znode = dictGetKey(de);
1755	
1756	        /* Delete from the hash table and later from the skiplist.
1757	         * Note that the order is important: deleting from the skiplist
1758	         * actually releases the SDS string representing the element,
1759	         * which is shared between the skiplist and the hash table, so
1760	         * we need to delete from the skiplist as the final step. */
1761	        dictFreeUnlinkedEntry(zs->dict,de);
1762	
1763	        /* Delete from skiplist. */
1764	        zslDelete(zs->zsl, znode);
1765	
1766	        return 1;
1767	    }
1768	
1769	    return 0;
1770	}
1771	
1772	/* Delete the element 'ele' from the sorted set, returning 1 if the element
1773	 * existed and was deleted, 0 otherwise (the element was not there). */
1774	int zsetDel(robj *zobj, sds ele) {
1775	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1776	        unsigned char *eptr;
1777	
1778	        if ((eptr = zzlFind(zobj->ptr,ele,NULL)) != NULL) {
1779	            zobj->ptr = zzlDelete(zobj->ptr,eptr);
1780	            return 1;
1781	        }
1782	    } else if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_SKIPLIST) {
1783	        zset *zs = zobj->ptr;
1784	        if (zsetRemoveFromSkiplist(zs, ele)) {
1785	            return 1;
1786	        }
1787	    } else {
1788	        serverPanic("Unknown sorted set encoding");
1789	    }
1790	    return 0; /* No such element found. */
1791	}
1792	
1793	/* Given a sorted set object returns the 0-based rank of the object or
1794	 * -1 if the object does not exist.
1795	 *
1796	 * For rank we mean the position of the element in the sorted collection
1797	 * of elements. So the first element has rank 0, the second rank 1, and so
1798	 * forth up to length-1 elements.
1799	 *
1800	 * If 'reverse' is false, the rank is returned considering as first element
1801	 * the one with the lowest score. Otherwise if 'reverse' is non-zero
1802	 * the rank is computed considering as element with rank 0 the one with
1803	 * the highest score. */
1804	long zsetRank(robj *zobj, sds ele, int reverse, double *output_score) {
1805	    unsigned long llen;
1806	    unsigned long rank;
1807	
1808	    llen = zsetLength(zobj);
1809	
1810	    if (zobj->encoding == OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK) {
1811	        unsigned char *zl = zobj->ptr;
1812	        unsigned char *eptr, *sptr;
1813	
1814	        eptr = lpSeek(zl,0);
1815	        serverAssert(eptr != NULL);
1816	        sptr = lpNext(zl,eptr);
1817	        serverAssert(sptr != NULL);
1818	        const size_t ele_len = sdslen(ele);

1820	        int cached_valid = 0;
