14	/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	 * Modules API documentation information
16	 *
17	 * The comments in this file are used to generate the API documentation on the
18	 * Redis website.
19	 *
20	 * Each function starting with RM_ and preceded by a block comment is included
21	 * in the API documentation. To hide an RM_ function, put a blank line between
22	 * the comment and the function definition or put the comment inside the
23	 * function body.
24	 *
25	 * The functions are divided into sections. Each section is preceded by a
26	 * documentation block, which is comment block starting with a markdown level 2
27	 * heading, i.e. a line starting with ##, on the first line of the comment block
28	 * (with the exception of a ----- line which can appear first). Other comment
29	 * blocks, which are not intended for the modules API user, such as this comment
30	 * block, do NOT start with a markdown level 2 heading, so they are included in
31	 * the generated a API documentation.
32	 *
33	 * The documentation comments may contain markdown formatting. Some automatic
34	 * replacements are done, such as the replacement of RM with RedisModule in
35	 * function names. For details, see the script src/modules/gendoc.rb.
36	 * -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
37	
38	#include "server.h"
39	#include "cluster.h"
40	#include "cluster_asm.h"
41	#include "slowlog.h"
42	#include "rdb.h"
43	#include "monotonic.h"
44	#include "script.h"
45	#include "call_reply.h"
46	#include "hdr_histogram.h"
47	#include "crc16_slottable.h"
48	#include <dlfcn.h>
49	#include <sys/stat.h>
50	#include <sys/wait.h>
51	#include <fcntl.h>
52	#include <string.h>
53	
54	/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	 * Private data structures used by the modules system. Those are data
56	 * structures that are never exposed to Redis Modules, if not as void
57	 * pointers that have an API the module can call with them)
58	 * -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
59	
60	struct RedisModuleInfoCtx {
61	    struct RedisModule *module;
62	    dict *requested_sections;
63	    sds info;           /* info string we collected so far */
64	    int sections;       /* number of sections we collected so far */
65	    int in_section;     /* indication if we're in an active section or not */
66	    int in_dict_field;  /* indication that we're currently appending to a dict */
67	};
68	
69	/* This represents a shared API. Shared APIs will be used to populate
70	 * the server.sharedapi dictionary, mapping names of APIs exported by
71	 * modules for other modules to use, to their structure specifying the
72	 * function pointer that can be called. */
73	struct RedisModuleSharedAPI {
74	    void *func;
75	    RedisModule *module;
76	};
77	typedef struct RedisModuleSharedAPI RedisModuleSharedAPI;
78	typedef struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx RedisModuleKeyOptCtx;
79	
80	dict *modules; /* Hash table of modules. SDS -> RedisModule ptr.*/
81	
82	/* Entries in the context->amqueue array, representing objects to free
83	 * when the callback returns. */
84	struct AutoMemEntry {
85	    void *ptr;
86	    int type;
87	};
88	
89	/* AutoMemEntry type field values. */
90	#define REDISMODULE_AM_KEY 0
91	#define REDISMODULE_AM_STRING 1
92	#define REDISMODULE_AM_REPLY 2
93	#define REDISMODULE_AM_FREED 3 /* Explicitly freed by user already. */
94	#define REDISMODULE_AM_DICT 4
95	#define REDISMODULE_AM_INFO 5
96	#define REDISMODULE_AM_CONFIG 6
97	#define REDISMODULE_AM_SLOTRANGEARRAY 7
98	
99	/* The pool allocator block. Redis Modules can allocate memory via this special
100	 * allocator that will automatically release it all once the callback returns.
101	 * This means that it can only be used for ephemeral allocations. However
102	 * there are two advantages for modules to use this API:
103	 *
104	 * 1) The memory is automatically released when the callback returns.
105	 * 2) This allocator is faster for many small allocations since whole blocks
106	 *    are allocated, and small pieces returned to the caller just advancing
107	 *    the index of the allocation.
108	 *
109	 * Allocations are always rounded to the size of the void pointer in order
110	 * to always return aligned memory chunks. */
111	
112	#define REDISMODULE_POOL_ALLOC_MIN_SIZE (1024*8)
113	#define REDISMODULE_POOL_ALLOC_ALIGN (sizeof(void*))
114	
115	typedef struct RedisModulePoolAllocBlock {
116	    uint32_t size;
117	    uint32_t used;
118	    struct RedisModulePoolAllocBlock *next;
119	    char memory[];
120	} RedisModulePoolAllocBlock;
121	
122	/* This structure represents the context in which Redis modules operate.
123	 * Most APIs module can access, get a pointer to the context, so that the API
124	 * implementation can hold state across calls, or remember what to free after
125	 * the call and so forth.
126	 *
127	 * Note that not all the context structure is always filled with actual values
128	 * but only the fields needed in a given context. */
129	
130	struct RedisModuleBlockedClient;
131	struct RedisModuleUser;
132	
133	struct RedisModuleCtx {
134	    void *getapifuncptr;            /* NOTE: Must be the first field. */
135	    struct RedisModule *module;     /* Module reference. */
136	    client *client;                 /* Client calling a command. */
137	    struct RedisModuleBlockedClient *blocked_client; /* Blocked client for
138	                                                        thread safe context. */
139	    struct AutoMemEntry *amqueue;   /* Auto memory queue of objects to free. */
140	    int amqueue_len;                /* Number of slots in amqueue. */
141	    int amqueue_used;               /* Number of used slots in amqueue. */
142	    int flags;                      /* REDISMODULE_CTX_... flags. */
143	    void **postponed_arrays;        /* To set with RM_ReplySetArrayLength(). */
144	    int postponed_arrays_count;     /* Number of entries in postponed_arrays. */
145	    void *blocked_privdata;         /* Privdata set when unblocking a client. */
146	    RedisModuleString *blocked_ready_key; /* Key ready when the reply callback
147	                                             gets called for clients blocked
148	                                             on keys. */
149	
150	    /* Used if there is the REDISMODULE_CTX_KEYS_POS_REQUEST or 
151	     * REDISMODULE_CTX_CHANNEL_POS_REQUEST flag set. */
152	    getKeysResult *keys_result;
153	
154	    struct RedisModulePoolAllocBlock *pa_head;
155	    long long next_yield_time;
156	
157	    const struct RedisModuleUser *user;  /* RedisModuleUser commands executed via
158	                                            RM_Call should be executed as, if set */
159	};
160	typedef struct RedisModuleCtx RedisModuleCtx;
161	
162	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_NONE (0)
163	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_AUTO_MEMORY (1<<0)
164	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_KEYS_POS_REQUEST (1<<1)
165	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_BLOCKED_REPLY (1<<2)
166	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_BLOCKED_TIMEOUT (1<<3)
167	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_THREAD_SAFE (1<<4)
168	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_BLOCKED_DISCONNECTED (1<<5)
169	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_TEMP_CLIENT (1<<6) /* Return client object to the pool
170	                                              when the context is destroyed */
171	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_NEW_CLIENT (1<<7)  /* Free client object when the
172	                                              context is destroyed */
173	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_CHANNELS_POS_REQUEST (1<<8)
174	#define REDISMODULE_CTX_COMMAND (1<<9) /* Context created to serve a command from call() or AOF (which calls cmd->proc directly) */
175	
176	
177	/* This represents a Redis key opened with RM_OpenKey(). */
178	struct RedisModuleKey {
179	    RedisModuleCtx *ctx;
180	    redisDb *db;
181	    robj *key;      /* Key name object. */
182	    kvobj *kv;      /* Key-Value object, or NULL if the key was not found. */
183	    void *iter;     /* Iterator. */
184	    int mode;       /* Opening mode. */
185	
186	    union {
187	        struct {
188	            /* List, use only if value->type == OBJ_LIST */
189	            listTypeEntry entry;   /* Current entry in iteration. */
190	            long index;            /* Current 0-based index in iteration. */
191	        } list;
192	        struct {
193	            /* Zset iterator, use only if value->type == OBJ_ZSET */
194	            uint32_t type;         /* REDISMODULE_ZSET_RANGE_* */
195	            zrangespec rs;         /* Score range. */
196	            zlexrangespec lrs;     /* Lex range. */
197	            uint32_t start;        /* Start pos for positional ranges. */
198	            uint32_t end;          /* End pos for positional ranges. */
199	            void *current;         /* Zset iterator current node. */
200	            int er;                /* Zset iterator end reached flag
201	                                       (true if end was reached). */
202	        } zset;
203	        struct {
204	            /* Stream, use only if value->type == OBJ_STREAM */
